query:
You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime repository.
The codebase is at version 0.6 (commit: cc8121a6).

## Issue to Resolve

Potential improvements and input preservation in parsing and formatting error messages
I've set the experiment "I have a system with externally-supplied input that I'm parsing" in order to see how debuggable the end system is.

Here are a few potential improvements that can be made:

1) `Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker` correctly shows an unexpected component, but it would be nice to see the full input to understand the source of the problem.

E.g. for `Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is 23, but the AM/PM marker is AM` `23` is nice, but for observability purposes, I would like to see the whole input.


2) `Position X: Expected One of` probably should be `Position 0: Expected one of`


3) The same as for `1` it goes for validation of components done in constructors: `LocalDate`, `LocalDateTime`, `LocalTime`, `UtcOffset`

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `core/common/src/LocalDate.kt`
- `core/common/src/LocalDateTime.kt`
- `core/common/src/LocalTime.kt`
- `core/common/src/UtcOffset.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/DateTimeComponents.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormat.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/format/FieldFormatDirective.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/format/FormatStructure.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/format/parser/ParserOperation.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1‚Üí/*
  2‚Üí * Copyright 2019-2022 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.

  4‚Üí */
  5‚Üí
  6‚Üípackage kotlinx.datetime
  7‚Üí
  8‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 10‚Üíimport kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11‚Üí
 12‚Üí/**
 13‚Üí * The date part of [LocalDateTime].
 14‚Üí *
 15‚Üí * This class represents dates without a reference to a particular time zone.
 16‚Üí * As such, these objects may denote different spans of time in different time zones: for someone in Berlin,
 17‚Üí * `2020-08-30` started and ended at different moments from those for someone in Tokyo.
 18‚Üí *
 19‚Üí * The arithmetic on [LocalDate] values is defined independently of the time zone (so `2020-08-30` plus one day
 20‚Üí * is `2020-08-31` everywhere): see various [LocalDate.plus] and [LocalDate.minus] functions, as well
 21‚Üí * as [LocalDate.periodUntil] and various other [*until][LocalDate.daysUntil] functions.
 22‚Üí */
 23‚Üí@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 24‚Üípublic expect class LocalDate : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 25‚Üí    public companion object {
 26‚Üí        /**
 27‚Üí         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 28‚Üí         *
 29‚Üí         * Parses a string that represents a date and returns the parsed [LocalDate] value.
 30‚Üí         *
 31‚Üí         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 32‚Üí         *
 33‚Üí         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalDate] are exceeded.
 34‚Üí         *
 35‚Üí         * @see LocalDate.toString for formatting using the default format.
 36‚Üí         * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
 37‚Üí         */
 38‚Üí        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = getIsoDateFormat()): LocalDate
 39‚Üí
 40‚Üí        /**
 41‚Üí         * Returns a [LocalDate] that is [epochDays] number of days from the epoch day `1970-01-01`.
 42‚Üí         *
 43‚Üí         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the platform-specific boundaries of [LocalDate].
 44‚Üí         *
 45‚Üí         * @see LocalDate.toEpochDays
 46‚Üí         */
 47‚Üí        public fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate
 48‚Üí
 49‚Üí        /**
 50‚Üí         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 51‚Üí         *
 52‚Üí         * Example:
 53‚Üí         * ```
 54‚Üí         * // 2020 Jan 05
 55‚Üí         * LocalDate.Format {
 56‚Üí         *   year()
 57‚Üí         *   char(' ')
 58‚Üí         *   monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
 59‚Üí         *   char(' ')
 60‚Üí         *   dayOfMonth()
 61‚Üí         * }
 62‚Üí         * ```
 63‚Üí         *
 64‚Üí         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
 65‚Üí         * (for example, [dayOfMonth] is 31 for February), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
 66‚Üí         *
 67‚Üí         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalDate.Formats].
 68‚Üí         */
 69‚Üí        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 70‚Üí        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 71‚Üí
 72‚Üí        internal val MIN: LocalDate
 73‚Üí        internal val MAX: LocalDate
 74‚Üí    }
 75‚Üí
 76‚Üí    /**
 77‚Üí     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 78‚Üí     *
 79‚Üí     * See [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] and [LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC] for popular predefined formats.
 80‚Üí     * [LocalDate.parse] and [LocalDate.toString] can be used as convenient shortcuts for the
 81‚Üí     * [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] format.
 82‚Üí     *
 83‚Üí     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalDate.Format] to create a custom
 84‚Üí     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalDate] values.
 85‚Üí     */
 86‚Üí    public object Formats {
 87‚Üí        /**
 88‚Üí         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [LocalDate.toString] and [LocalDate.parse].
 89‚Üí         *
 90‚Üí         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 format:
 91‚Üí         * - `2020-08-30`
 92‚Üí         * - `+12020-08-30`
 93‚Üí         * - `0000-08-30`
 94‚Üí         * - `-0001-08-30`
 95‚Üí         */
 96‚Üí        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 97‚Üí
 98‚Üí        /**
 99‚Üí         * ISO 8601 basic format.
100‚Üí         *
101‚Üí         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 basic format:
102‚Üí         * - `20200830`
103‚Üí         * - `+120200830`
104‚Üí         * - `00000830`
105‚Üí         * - `-00010830`
106‚Üí         */
107‚Üí        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
108‚Üí    }
109‚Üí
110‚Üí    /**
111‚Üí     * Constructs a [LocalDate] instance from the given date components.
112‚Üí     *
113‚Üí     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
114‚Üí     *
115‚Üí     * The supported ranges of components:
116‚Üí     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
117‚Üí     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
118‚Üí     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`
119‚Üí     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
120‚Üí     *
121‚Üí     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the
122‚Üí     * given [monthNumber] and [year].
123‚Üí     */
124‚Üí    public constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int)
125‚Üí
126‚Üí    /**
127‚Üí     * Constructs a [LocalDate] instance from the given date components.
128‚Üí     *
129‚Üí     * The supported ranges of components:
130‚Üí     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
131‚Üí     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
132‚Üí     * - [month] all values of the [Month] enum
133‚Üí     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
134‚Üí     *
135‚Üí     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the
136‚Üí     * given [month] and [year].
137‚Üí     */
138‚Üí    public constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int)
139‚Üí
140‚Üí    /** Returns the year component of the date. */
141‚Üí    public val year: Int
142‚Üí
143‚Üí    /** Returns the number-of-month (1..12) component of the date. */
144‚Üí    public val monthNumber: Int
145‚Üí
146‚Üí    /** Returns the month ([Month]) component of the date. */
147‚Üí    public val month: Month
148‚Üí
149‚Üí    /** Returns the day-of-month component of the date. */
150‚Üí    public val dayOfMonth: Int
151‚Üí
152‚Üí    /** Returns the day-of-week component of the date. */
153‚Üí    public val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek
154‚Üí
155‚Üí    /** Returns the day-of-year component of the date. */
156‚Üí    public val dayOfYear: Int
157‚Üí
158‚Üí    /**
159‚Üí     * Returns the number of days since the epoch day `1970-01-01`.
160‚Üí     *
161‚Üí     * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
162‚Üí     *
163‚Üí     * @see LocalDate.fromEpochDays
164‚Üí     */
165‚Üí    public fun toEpochDays(): Int
166‚Üí
167‚Üí    /**
168‚Üí     * Compares `this` date with the [other] date.
169‚Üí     * Returns zero if this date represent the same day as the other (i.e. equal to other),
170‚Üí     * a negative number if this date is earlier than the other,
171‚Üí     * and a positive number if this date is later than the other.
172‚Üí     */
173‚Üí    public override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int
174‚Üí
175‚Üí    /**
176‚Üí     * Converts this date to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
177‚Üí     *
178‚Üí     * @see Formats.ISO for the format details.
179‚Üí     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalDate] from a string.
180‚Üí     * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
181‚Üí     */
182‚Üí    public override fun toString(): String
183‚Üí}
184‚Üí
185‚Üí/**
186‚Üí * Formats this value using the given [format].
187‚Üí * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
188‚Üí */
189‚Üípublic fun LocalDate.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): String = format.format(this)
190‚Üí
191‚Üí/**
192‚Üí * @suppress
193‚Üí */
194‚Üí@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("LocalDate.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
195‚Üípublic fun String.toLocalDate(): LocalDate = LocalDate.parse(this)
196‚Üí
197‚Üí/**
198‚Üí * Combines this date's components with the specified time components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
199‚Üí *
200‚Üí * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone consider using
201‚Üí * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
202‚Üí */
203‚Üípublic fun LocalDate.atTime(hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int = 0, nanosecond: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
204‚Üí    LocalDateTime(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
205‚Üí
206‚Üí/**
207‚Üí * Combines this date's components with the specified [LocalTime] components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
208‚Üí *
209‚Üí * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone consider using
210‚Üí * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
211‚Üí */
212‚Üípublic fun LocalDate.atTime(time: LocalTime): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(this, time)
213‚Üí
214‚Üí
215‚Üí/**
216‚Üí * Returns a date that is the result of adding components of [DatePeriod] to this date. The components are
217‚Üí * added in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to days.
218‚Üí *
219‚Üí * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
220‚Üí * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
221‚Üí * [LocalDate].
222‚Üí */
223‚Üípublic expect operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate
224‚Üí
225‚Üí/**
226‚Üí * Returns a date that is the result of subtracting components of [DatePeriod] from this date. The components are
227‚Üí * subtracted in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to days.
228‚Üí *
229‚Üí * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
230‚Üí * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
231‚Üí * [LocalDate].
232‚Üí */
233‚Üípublic operator fun LocalDate.minus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate =
234‚Üí    if (period.days != Int.MIN_VALUE && period.months != Int.MIN_VALUE) {
235‚Üí        plus(with(period) { DatePeriod(-years, -months, -days) })
236‚Üí    } else {
237‚Üí        minus(period.years, DateTimeUnit.YEAR).minus(period.months, DateTimeUnit.MONTH)
238‚Üí            .minus(period.days, DateTimeUnit.DAY)
239‚Üí    }
240‚Üí
241‚Üí/**
242‚Üí * Returns a [DatePeriod] representing the difference between `this` and [other] dates.
243‚Üí *
244‚Üí * The components of [DatePeriod] are calculated so that adding it to `this` date results in the [other] date.
245‚Üí *
246‚Üí * All components of the [DatePeriod] returned are:
247‚Üí * - positive or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
248‚Üí * - negative or zero if this date is later than the other,
249‚Üí * - exactly zero if this date is equal to the other.
250‚Üí *
251‚Üí * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int (JVM only).
252‚Üí *
253‚Üí * @see LocalDate.minus
254‚Üí */
255‚Üípublic expect fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod
256‚Üí
257‚Üí/**
258‚Üí * Returns a [DatePeriod] representing the difference between [other] and `this` dates.
259‚Üí *
260‚Üí * The components of [DatePeriod] are calculated so that adding it back to the `other` date results in this date.
261‚Üí *
262‚Üí * All components of the [DatePeriod] returned are:
263‚Üí * - negative or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
264‚Üí * - positive or zero if this date is later than the other,
265‚Üí * - exactly zero if this date is equal to the other.
266‚Üí *
267‚Üí * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int (JVM only).
268‚Üí *
269‚Üí * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
270‚Üí */
271‚Üípublic operator fun LocalDate.minus(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod = other.periodUntil(this)
272‚Üí
273‚Üí/**
274‚Üí * Returns the whole number of the specified date [units][unit] between `this` and [other] dates.
275‚Üí *
276‚Üí * The value returned is:
277‚Üí * - positive or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
278‚Üí * - negative or zero if this date is later than the other,
279‚Üí * - zero if this date is equal to the other.
280‚Üí
281‚Üí * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
282‚Üí *
283‚Üí * @see LocalDate.daysUntil
284‚Üí * @see LocalDate.monthsUntil
285‚Üí * @see LocalDate.yearsUntil
286‚Üí *
287‚Üí */
288‚Üípublic expect fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int
289‚Üí
290‚Üí/**
291‚Üí * Returns the number of whole days between two dates.
292‚Üí *
293‚Üí * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
294‚Üí *
295‚Üí * @see LocalDate.until
296‚Üí */
297‚Üípublic expect fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
298‚Üí
299‚Üí/**
300‚Üí * Returns the number of whole months between two dates.
301‚Üí *
302‚Üí * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
303‚Üí *
304‚Üí * @see LocalDate.until
305‚Üí */
306‚Üípublic expect fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
307‚Üí
308‚Üí/**
309‚Üí * Returns the number of whole years between two dates.
310‚Üí *
311‚Üí * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
312‚Üí *
313‚Üí * @see LocalDate.until
314‚Üí */
315‚Üípublic expect fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
316‚Üí
317‚Üí/**
318‚Üí * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of adding one [unit] to this date.
319‚Üí *
320‚Üí * The returned date is later than this date.
321‚Üí *
322‚Üí * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
323‚Üí */
324‚Üí@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
325‚Üípublic expect fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
326‚Üí
327‚Üí/**
328‚Üí * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of subtracting one [unit] from this date.
329‚Üí *

333‚Üí */
334‚Üí@Deprecated("Use the minus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.minus(1, unit)"))
335‚Üípublic fun LocalDate.minus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plus(-1, unit)
336‚Üí

339‚Üí *

344‚Üí */
345‚Üípublic expect fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate

349‚Üí *

351‚Üí * If the [value] is negative, the returned date is later than this date.
352‚Üí *
353‚Üí * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
354‚Üí */
355‚Üípublic expect fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
356‚Üí
357‚Üí/**
358‚Üí * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of adding the [value] number of the specified [unit] to this date.
359‚Üí *
360‚Üí * If the [value] is positive, the returned date is later than this date.
361‚Üí * If the [value] is negative, the returned date is earlier than this date.
362‚Üí *
363‚Üí * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
364‚Üí */
365‚Üípublic expect fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
366‚Üí

369‚Üí *

372‚Üí *

374‚Üí */
375‚Üípublic fun LocalDate.minus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plus(-value, unit)

  6‚Üípackage kotlinx.datetime
  7‚Üí
  8‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate.Companion.parse

 10‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer
 11‚Üíimport kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 12‚Üí
 13‚Üí/**
 14‚Üí * The representation of a specific civil date and time without a reference to a particular time zone.
 15‚Üí *
 16‚Üí * This class does not describe specific *moments in time*, which are represented as [Instant] values.
 17‚Üí * Instead, its instances can be thought of as clock readings, something that an observer in a particular time zone
 18‚Üí * could witness.
 19‚Üí * For example, `2020-08-30T18:43` is not a *moment in time*, since someone in Berlin and someone in Tokyo would witness
 20‚Üí * this on their clocks at different times.
 21‚Üí *
 22‚Üí * The main purpose of this class is to provide human-readable representations of [Instant] values, or to transfer them
 23‚Üí * as data.
 24‚Üí *
 25‚Üí * The arithmetic on [LocalDateTime] values is not provided, since without accounting for the time zone transitions it may give misleading results.
 26‚Üí */
 27‚Üí@Serializable(with = LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer::class)
 28‚Üípublic expect class LocalDateTime : Comparable<LocalDateTime> {
 29‚Üí    public companion object {
 30‚Üí
 31‚Üí        /**
 32‚Üí         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 33‚Üí         *
 34‚Üí         * Parses a string that represents a date/time value including date and time components
 35‚Üí         * but without any time zone component and returns the parsed [LocalDateTime] value.
 36‚Üí         *
 37‚Üí         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 38‚Üí         *
 39‚Üí         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalDateTime] are
 40‚Üí         * exceeded.
 41‚Üí         */
 42‚Üí        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime> = getIsoDateTimeFormat()): LocalDateTime
 43‚Üí
 44‚Üí        /**
 45‚Üí         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalDateTime] values.
 46‚Üí         *
 47‚Üí         * Examples:
 48‚Üí         * ```
 49‚Üí         * // `2020-08-30 18:43:13`, using predefined date and time formats
 50‚Üí         * LocalDateTime.Format { date(LocalDate.Formats.ISO); char(' '); time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO) }
 51‚Üí         *
 52‚Üí         * // `08/30 18:43:13`, using a custom format:
 53‚Üí         * LocalDateTime.Format {
 54‚Üí         *   monthNumber(); char('/'); dayOfMonth()
 55‚Üí         *   char(' ')
 56‚Üí         *   hour(); char(':'); minute()
 57‚Üí         *   optional { char(':'); second() }
 58‚Üí         * }
 59‚Üí         * ```
 60‚Üí         *
 61‚Üí         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
 62‚Üí         * (for example, [dayOfMonth] is 31 for February), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
 63‚Üí         *
 64‚Üí         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalDateTime.Formats].
 65‚Üí         */
 66‚Üí        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 67‚Üí        public fun Format(builder: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>
 68‚Üí
 69‚Üí        internal val MIN: LocalDateTime
 70‚Üí        internal val MAX: LocalDateTime
 71‚Üí    }
 72‚Üí
 73‚Üí    /**
 74‚Üí     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDateTime] values.
 75‚Üí     *
 76‚Üí     * [LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO] is a popular predefined format.
 77‚Üí     *
 78‚Üí     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalDateTime.Format] to create a custom
 79‚Üí     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalDateTime] values.
 80‚Üí     */
 81‚Üí    public object Formats {
 82‚Üí        /**
 83‚Üí         * ISO 8601 extended format.
 84‚Üí         *
 85‚Üí         * Examples of date/time in ISO 8601 format:
 86‚Üí         * - `2020-08-30T18:43`
 87‚Üí         * - `+12020-08-30T18:43:00`
 88‚Üí         * - `0000-08-30T18:43:00.5`
 89‚Üí         * - `-0001-08-30T18:43:00.123456789`
 90‚Üí         *
 91‚Üí         * When formatting, seconds are always included, even if they are zero.
 92‚Üí         * Fractional parts of the second are included if non-zero.
 93‚Üí         *
 94‚Üí         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [LocalDateTime.toString] produces.
 95‚Üí         */
 96‚Üí        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>
 97‚Üí    }
 98‚Üí
 99‚Üí    /**
100‚Üí     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance from the given date and time components.
101‚Üí     *
102‚Üí     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
103‚Üí     *
104‚Üí     * The supported ranges of components:
105‚Üí     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
106‚Üí     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
107‚Üí     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`
108‚Üí     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
109‚Üí     * - [hour] `0..23`
110‚Üí     * - [minute] `0..59`
111‚Üí     * - [second] `0..59`
112‚Üí     * - [nanosecond] `0..999_999_999`
113‚Üí     *
114‚Üí     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the given [monthNumber] and
115‚Üí     * [year].
116‚Üí     */
117‚Üí    public constructor(
118‚Üí        year: Int,
119‚Üí        monthNumber: Int,
120‚Üí        dayOfMonth: Int,
121‚Üí        hour: Int,
122‚Üí        minute: Int,
123‚Üí        second: Int = 0,
124‚Üí        nanosecond: Int = 0
125‚Üí    )
126‚Üí
127‚Üí    /**
128‚Üí     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance from the given date and time components.
129‚Üí     *
130‚Üí     * The supported ranges of components:
131‚Üí     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
132‚Üí     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
133‚Üí     * - [month] all values of the [Month] enum
134‚Üí     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
135‚Üí     * - [hour] `0..23`
136‚Üí     * - [minute] `0..59`
137‚Üí     * - [second] `0..59`
138‚Üí     * - [nanosecond] `0..999_999_999`
139‚Üí     *
140‚Üí     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the given [month] and
141‚Üí     * [year].
142‚Üí     */
143‚Üí    public constructor(
144‚Üí        year: Int,
145‚Üí        month: Month,
146‚Üí        dayOfMonth: Int,
147‚Üí        hour: Int,
148‚Üí        minute: Int,
149‚Üí        second: Int = 0,
150‚Üí        nanosecond: Int = 0
151‚Üí    )
152‚Üí
153‚Üí    /**
154‚Üí     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance by combining the given [date] and [time] parts.
155‚Üí     */
156‚Üí    public constructor(date: LocalDate, time: LocalTime)
157‚Üí
158‚Üí    /** Returns the year component of the date. */
159‚Üí    public val year: Int
160‚Üí
161‚Üí    /** Returns the number-of-month (1..12) component of the date. */
162‚Üí    public val monthNumber: Int
163‚Üí
164‚Üí    /** Returns the month ([Month]) component of the date. */
165‚Üí    public val month: Month
166‚Üí
167‚Üí    /** Returns the day-of-month component of the date. */
168‚Üí    public val dayOfMonth: Int
169‚Üí
170‚Üí    /** Returns the day-of-week component of the date. */
171‚Üí    public val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek
172‚Üí
173‚Üí    /** Returns the day-of-year component of the date. */
174‚Üí    public val dayOfYear: Int
175‚Üí
176‚Üí    /** Returns the hour-of-day time component of this date/time value. */
177‚Üí    public val hour: Int
178‚Üí
179‚Üí    /** Returns the minute-of-hour time component of this date/time value. */
180‚Üí    public val minute: Int
181‚Üí
182‚Üí    /** Returns the second-of-minute time component of this date/time value. */
183‚Üí    public val second: Int
184‚Üí
185‚Üí    /** Returns the nanosecond-of-second time component of this date/time value. */
186‚Üí    public val nanosecond: Int
187‚Üí
188‚Üí    /** Returns the date part of this date/time value. */
189‚Üí    public val date: LocalDate
190‚Üí
191‚Üí    /** Returns the time part of this date/time value. */
192‚Üí    public val time: LocalTime
193‚Üí
194‚Üí    /**
195‚Üí     * Compares `this` date/time value with the [other] date/time value.
196‚Üí     * Returns zero if this value is equal to the other,
197‚Üí     * a negative number if this value represents earlier civil time than the other,
198‚Üí     * and a positive number if this value represents later civil time than the other.
199‚Üí     */
200‚Üí    // TODO: add a note about pitfalls of comparing localdatetimes falling in the Autumn transition
201‚Üí    public override operator fun compareTo(other: LocalDateTime): Int
202‚Üí
203‚Üí    /**
204‚Üí     * Converts this date/time value to the ISO 8601 string representation.
205‚Üí     *
206‚Üí     * For readability, if the time represents a round minute (without seconds or fractional seconds),
207‚Üí     * the string representation will not include seconds. Also, fractions of seconds will add trailing zeros to
208‚Üí     * the fractional part until its length is a multiple of three.

210‚Üí     * Examples of output:
211‚Üí     * - `2020-08-30T18:43`
212‚Üí     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00`
213‚Üí     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.500`
214‚Üí     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789`

216‚Üí     * @see LocalTime.toString for details of how the time part is formatted.
217‚Üí     * @see Formats.ISO for a very similar format. The difference is that [Formats.ISO] will always include seconds,

224‚Üí
225‚Üí/**

229‚Üípublic fun LocalDateTime.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>): String = format.format(this)

 12‚Üí
 13‚Üí
 14‚Üí/**

 19‚Üí * combined with [LocalDate] via [LocalDate.atTime] or [LocalTime.atDate].

 22‚Üí * Therefore, even on the same date, [LocalTime] denotes different moments of time.
 23‚Üí * For example, `18:43` happens at different moments in Berlin and in Tokyo.

 26‚Üí * transitions it may give misleading results.

 30‚Üí    public companion object {

 45‚Üí        /**

 47‚Üí         * The fractional parts of the second will be zero.

 50‚Üí         * with 86400 being the number of seconds in a calendar day.

 54‚Üí         * @see LocalTime.fromNanosecondOfDay

 57‚Üí
 58‚Üí        /**

 60‚Üí         * a calendar day.

 74‚Üí         * a calendar day.

108‚Üí    /**

135‚Üí     * - [minute] `0..59`

137‚Üí     * - [nanosecond] `0..999_999_999`

210‚Üí * Combines this time's components with the specified date components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
211‚Üí */
212‚Üípublic fun LocalTime.atDate(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
213‚Üí    LocalDateTime(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
214‚Üí
215‚Üí/**
216‚Üí * Combines this time's components with the specified date components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
217‚Üí */
218‚Üípublic fun LocalTime.atDate(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
219‚Üí    LocalDateTime(year, month, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
220‚Üí
221‚Üí/**
222‚Üí * Combines this time's components with the specified [LocalDate] components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
223‚Üí */
224‚Üípublic fun LocalTime.atDate(date: LocalDate): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(date, this)
225‚Üí
226‚Üí// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
227‚Üíinternal fun getIsoTimeFormat() = LocalTime.Formats.ISO

  6‚Üípackage kotlinx.datetime
  7‚Üí
  8‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.serializers.UtcOffsetSerializer
 10‚Üíimport kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11‚Üí
 12‚Üí/**
 13‚Üí * An offset from UTC.
 14‚Üí *
 15‚Üí * Examples of these values:
 16‚Üí * - `Z`, an offset of zero;
 17‚Üí * - `+05`, plus five hours;
 18‚Üí * - `-02`, minus two hours;
 19‚Üí * - `+03:30`, plus three hours and thirty minutes;
 20‚Üí * - `+01:23:45`, plus one hour, 23 minutes, and 45 seconds.
 21‚Üí */
 22‚Üí@Serializable(with = UtcOffsetSerializer::class)
 23‚Üípublic expect class UtcOffset {
 24‚Üí    /**
 25‚Üí     * The number of seconds from UTC.
 26‚Üí     *
 27‚Üí     * The larger the value, the earlier some specific civil date/time happens with the offset.
 28‚Üí     */
 29‚Üí    public val totalSeconds: Int
 30‚Üí
 31‚Üí    // TODO: Declare and document toString/equals/hashCode
 32‚Üí
 33‚Üí    public companion object {
 34‚Üí        /**
 35‚Üí         * The zero offset from UTC, `Z`.
 36‚Üí         */
 37‚Üí        public val ZERO: UtcOffset
 38‚Üí
 39‚Üí        /**
 40‚Üí         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 41‚Üí         *
 42‚Üí         * Parses a string that represents a UTC offset and returns the parsed [UtcOffset] value.
 43‚Üí         *
 44‚Üí         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 45‚Üí         *
 46‚Üí         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [UtcOffset] are
 47‚Üí         * exceeded.
 48‚Üí         */
 49‚Üí        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset> = getIsoUtcOffestFormat()): UtcOffset
 50‚Üí
 51‚Üí        /**
 52‚Üí         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [UtcOffset] values.
 53‚Üí         *
 54‚Üí         * Example:
 55‚Üí         * ```
 56‚Üí         * // `GMT` on zero, `+4:30:15`, using a custom format:
 57‚Üí         * UtcOffset.Format {
 58‚Üí         *   optional("GMT") {
 59‚Üí         *     offsetHours(Padding.NONE); char(':'); offsetMinutesOfHour()
 60‚Üí         *     optional { char(':'); offsetSecondsOfMinute() }
 61‚Üí         *   }
 62‚Üí         * }
 63‚Üí         * ```
 64‚Üí         *
 65‚Üí         * Since [UtcOffset] values are rarely formatted and parsed on their own,
 66‚Üí         * instances of [DateTimeFormat] obtained here will likely need to be passed to
 67‚Üí         * [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset.offset] in a format builder for a larger data structure.
 68‚Üí         *
 69‚Üí         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [UtcOffset.Formats].
 70‚Üí         */
 71‚Üí        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 72‚Üí        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
 73‚Üí    }
 74‚Üí
 75‚Üí    /**
 76‚Üí     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [UtcOffset] values.
 77‚Üí     *
 78‚Üí     * See [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO], [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC], and [UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS]
 79‚Üí     * for popular predefined formats.
 80‚Üí     *
 81‚Üí     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [UtcOffset.Format] to create a custom
 82‚Üí     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [UtcOffset] values.
 83‚Üí     */
 84‚Üí    public object Formats {
 85‚Üí        /**
 86‚Üí         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [UtcOffset.parse] and [UtcOffset.toString].
 87‚Üí         *
 88‚Üí         * An extension of the ISO 8601 is that this format allows parsing and formatting seconds.
 89‚Üí         *
 90‚Üí         * When formatting, seconds are omitted if they are zero. If the whole offset is zero, the letter `Z` is output.
 91‚Üí         *
 92‚Üí         * Examples of UTC offsets in ISO 8601 format:
 93‚Üí         * - `Z` or `+00:00`, an offset of zero;
 94‚Üí         * - `+05:00`, five hours;
 95‚Üí         * - `-02:00`, minus two hours;
 96‚Üí         * - `-17:16`
 97‚Üí         * - `+10:36:30`
 98‚Üí         */
 99‚Üí        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
100‚Üí
101‚Üí        /**
102‚Üí         * ISO 8601 basic format.
103‚Üí         *
104‚Üí         * An extension of the ISO 8601 is that this format allows parsing and formatting seconds.
105‚Üí         *
106‚Üí         * When formatting, seconds are omitted if they are zero. If the whole offset is zero, the letter `Z` is output.
107‚Üí         *
108‚Üí         * Examples of UTC offsets in ISO 8601 basic format:
109‚Üí         * - `Z`
110‚Üí         * - `+05`
111‚Üí         * - `-1716`
112‚Üí         * - `+103630`
113‚Üí         *
114‚Üí         * @see UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS
115‚Üí         */
116‚Üí        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
117‚Üí
118‚Üí        /**
119‚Üí         * A subset of the ISO 8601 basic format that always outputs and parses exactly a numeric sign and four digits:
120‚Üí         * two digits for the hour and two digits for the minute. If the offset has a non-zero number of seconds,
121‚Üí         * they are truncated.
122‚Üí         *
123‚Üí         * Examples of UTC offsets in this format:
124‚Üí         * - `+0000`
125‚Üí         * - `+0500`
126‚Üí         * - `-1716`
127‚Üí         * - `+1036`
128‚Üí         *
129‚Üí         * @see UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC
130‚Üí         */
131‚Üí        public val FOUR_DIGITS: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
132‚Üí    }
133‚Üí
134‚Üí    /**
135‚Üí     * Converts this UTC offset to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
136‚Üí     *
137‚Üí     * @see Formats.ISO for the format details.
138‚Üí     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [UtcOffset] from a string.
139‚Üí     * @see UtcOffset.format for formatting using a custom format.
140‚Üí     */
141‚Üí    public override fun toString(): String
142‚Üí}
143‚Üí
144‚Üí/**
145‚Üí * Formats this value using the given [format].
146‚Üí * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
147‚Üí */
148‚Üípublic fun UtcOffset.format(format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>): String = format.format(this)
149‚Üí
150‚Üí/**
151‚Üí * Constructs a [UtcOffset] from hours, minutes, and seconds components.
152‚Üí *
153‚Üí * All components must have the same sign.
154‚Üí *
155‚Üí * The bounds are checked: it is invalid to pass something other than `¬±[0; 59]` as the number of seconds or minutes.
156‚Üí * For example, `UtcOffset(hours = 3, minutes = 61)` is invalid.
157‚Üí *
158‚Üí * However, the non-null component of the highest order can exceed these bounds,
159‚Üí * for example, `UtcOffset(minutes = 241)` is valid.
160‚Üí *
161‚Üí * @throws IllegalArgumentException if a component exceeds its bounds when a higher order component is specified.
162‚Üí * @throws IllegalArgumentException if components have different signs.
163‚Üí * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the resulting `UtcOffset` value is outside of range `¬±18:00`.
164‚Üí */
165‚Üípublic expect fun UtcOffset(hours: Int? = null, minutes: Int? = null, seconds: Int? = null): UtcOffset
166‚Üí
167‚Üí@Deprecated("Use UtcOffset.ZERO instead", ReplaceWith("UtcOffset.ZERO"), DeprecationLevel.ERROR)
168‚Üípublic fun UtcOffset(): UtcOffset = UtcOffset.ZERO
169‚Üí
170‚Üí/**
171‚Üí * Returns the fixed-offset time zone with the given UTC offset.
172‚Üí */
173‚Üípublic fun UtcOffset.asTimeZone(): FixedOffsetTimeZone = FixedOffsetTimeZone(this)
174‚Üí
175‚Üí// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
176‚Üíinternal fun getIsoUtcOffestFormat() = UtcOffset.Formats.ISO
177‚Üí

1‚Üí/*
  2‚Üí * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.

  5‚Üí
  6‚Üípackage kotlinx.datetime.format
  7‚Üí
  8‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.*
  9‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 10‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11‚Üí
 12‚Üí/**
 13‚Üí * A format for parsing and formatting date-time-related values.
 14‚Üí */
 15‚Üípublic sealed interface DateTimeFormat<T> {
 16‚Üí    /**
 17‚Üí     * Formats the given [value] into a string, using this format.
 18‚Üí     */
 19‚Üí    public fun format(value: T): String
 20‚Üí
 21‚Üí    /**
 22‚Üí     * Formats the given [value] into the given [appendable] using this format.
 23‚Üí     */
 24‚Üí    public fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A
 25‚Üí
 26‚Üí    /**
 27‚Üí     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 28‚Üí     *
 29‚Üí     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 30‚Üí     */
 31‚Üí    public fun parse(input: CharSequence): T
 32‚Üí
 33‚Üí    /**
 34‚Üí     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 35‚Üí     *
 36‚Üí     * @return the parsed value, or `null` if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 37‚Üí     */
 38‚Üí    public fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T?
 39‚Üí
 40‚Üí    public companion object {
 41‚Üí        /**
 42‚Üí         * Produces Kotlin code that, when pasted into a Kotlin source file, creates a [DateTimeFormat] instance that
 43‚Üí         * behaves identically to [format].
 44‚Üí         *
 45‚Üí         * The typical use case for this is to create a [DateTimeFormat] instance using a non-idiomatic approach and
 46‚Üí         * then convert it to a builder DSL.
 47‚Üí         */
 48‚Üí        public fun formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): String = when (format) {
 49‚Üí            is AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *> -> format.actualFormat.builderString(allFormatConstants)
 50‚Üí        }
 51‚Üí    }
 52‚Üí}
 53‚Üí
 54‚Üí/**
 55‚Üí * The style of padding to use when formatting a value.
 56‚Üí */
 57‚Üípublic enum class Padding {
 58‚Üí    /**
 59‚Üí     * No padding.
 60‚Üí     */
 61‚Üí    NONE,
 62‚Üí
 63‚Üí    /**
 64‚Üí     * Pad with zeros.
 65‚Üí     */
 66‚Üí    ZERO,
 67‚Üí
 68‚Üí    /**
 69‚Üí     * Pad with spaces.
 70‚Üí     */
 71‚Üí    SPACE
 72‚Üí}
 73‚Üí
 74‚Üíinternal fun Padding.toKotlinCode(): String = when (this) {
 75‚Üí    Padding.NONE -> "Padding.NONE"
 76‚Üí    Padding.ZERO -> "Padding.ZERO"
 77‚Üí    Padding.SPACE -> "Padding.SPACE"
 78‚Üí}
 79‚Üí
 80‚Üíinternal inline fun Padding.minDigits(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.ZERO) width else 1
 81‚Üíinternal inline fun Padding.spaces(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.SPACE) width else null
 82‚Üí
 83‚Üí/** [T] is the user-visible type, whereas [U] is its mutable representation for parsing and formatting. */
 84‚Üíinternal sealed class AbstractDateTimeFormat<T, U : Copyable<U>> : DateTimeFormat<T> {
 85‚Üí
 86‚Üí    abstract val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<U>
 87‚Üí
 88‚Üí    abstract fun intermediateFromValue(value: T): U
 89‚Üí
 90‚Üí    abstract fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: U): T
 91‚Üí
 92‚Üí    abstract val emptyIntermediate: U // should be part of the `Copyable` interface once the language allows this
 93‚Üí
 94‚Üí    open fun valueFromIntermediateOrNull(intermediate: U): T? = try {
 95‚Üí        valueFromIntermediate(intermediate)
 96‚Üí    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 97‚Üí        null
 98‚Üí    }
 99‚Üí
100‚Üí    override fun format(value: T): String = StringBuilder().also {
101‚Üí        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), it)
102‚Üí    }.toString()
103‚Üí
104‚Üí    override fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A = appendable.apply {
105‚Üí        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), this)
106‚Üí    }
107‚Üí
108‚Üí    override fun parse(input: CharSequence): T {
109‚Üí        val matched = try {
110‚Üí            // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
111‚Üí            Parser(actualFormat.parser()).match(input, emptyIntermediate)
112‚Üí        } catch (e: ParseException) {
113‚Üí            throw DateTimeFormatException("Failed to parse value from '$input'", e)
114‚Üí        }
115‚Üí        try {
116‚Üí            return valueFromIntermediate(matched)
117‚Üí        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
118‚Üí            throw DateTimeFormatException(e.message!!)
119‚Üí        }
120‚Üí    }
121‚Üí
122‚Üí    override fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T? =
123‚Üí        // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
124‚Üí        Parser(actualFormat.parser()).matchOrNull(input, emptyIntermediate)?.let { valueFromIntermediateOrNull(it) }
125‚Üí
126‚Üí}
127‚Üí
128‚Üíprivate val allFormatConstants: List<Pair<String, CachedFormatStructure<*>>> by lazy {
129‚Üí    fun unwrap(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): CachedFormatStructure<*> = (format as AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *>).actualFormat
130‚Üí    // the formats are ordered vaguely by decreasing length, as the topmost among suitable ones is chosen.
131‚Üí    listOf(
132‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123)" to
133‚Üí            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123),
134‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET)" to
135‚Üí            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET),
136‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime::date.name}(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO),
137‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO),
138‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
139‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::time.name}(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalTime.Formats.ISO),
140‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO),
141‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
142‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS),
143‚Üí    )
144‚Üí}
145‚Üí

1‚Üí/*
  2‚Üí * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3‚Üí * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4‚Üí */
  5‚Üí
  6‚Üípackage kotlinx.datetime.format
  7‚Üí
  8‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.*
  9‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.DayOfWeek
 10‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 11‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 12‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.Copyable
 13‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 14‚Üíimport kotlin.reflect.*
 15‚Üí
 16‚Üí/**
 17‚Üí * A collection of date-time fields, used specifically for parsing and formatting.
 18‚Üí *
 19‚Üí * Its main purpose is to provide support for complex date-time formats that don't correspond to any of the standard
 20‚Üí * entities in the library. For example, a format that includes only the month and the day of the month, but not the
 21‚Üí * year, can not be represented and parsed as a [LocalDate], but it is valid for a [DateTimeComponents].
 22‚Üí *
 23‚Üí * Example:
 24‚Üí * ```
 25‚Üí * val input = "2020-03-16T23:59:59.999999999+03:00"
 26‚Üí * val components = DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET.parse(input)
 27‚Üí * val localDateTime = components.toLocalDateTime() // LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999)
 28‚Üí * val instant = components.toInstantUsingOffset() // Instant.parse("2020-03-16T20:59:59.999999999Z")
 29‚Üí * val offset = components.toUtcOffset() // UtcOffset(hours = 3)
 30‚Üí * ```
 31‚Üí *
 32‚Üí * Another purpose is to support parsing and formatting data with out-of-bounds values. For example, parsing
 33‚Üí * `23:59:60` as a [LocalTime] is not possible, but it is possible to parse it as a [DateTimeComponents], adjust the value by
 34‚Üí * setting [second] to `59`, and then convert it to a [LocalTime] via [toLocalTime].
 35‚Üí *
 36‚Üí * Example:
 37‚Üí * ```
 38‚Üí * val input = "23:59:60"
 39‚Üí * val extraDay: Boolean
 40‚Üí * val time = DateTimeComponents.Format {
 41‚Üí *   time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)
 42‚Üí * }.parse(input).apply {
 43‚Üí *   if (hour == 23 && minute == 59 && second == 60) {
 44‚Üí *     hour = 0; minute = 0; second = 0; extraDay = true
 45‚Üí *   } else {
 46‚Üí *     extraDay = false
 47‚Üí *   }
 48‚Üí * }.toLocalTime()
 49‚Üí * ```
 50‚Üí *
 51‚Üí * Because this class has limited applications, constructing it directly is not possible.
 52‚Üí * For formatting, use the [format] overload that accepts a lambda with a [DateTimeComponents] receiver.
 53‚Üí *
 54‚Üí * Example:
 55‚Üí * ```
 56‚Üí * // Mon, 16 Mar 2020 23:59:59 +0300
 57‚Üí * DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123.format {
 58‚Üí *    setDateTimeOffset(LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999))
 59‚Üí *    setDateTimeOffset(UtcOffset(hours = 3))
 60‚Üí * }
 61‚Üí * ```
 62‚Üí *
 63‚Üí * Accessing the fields of this class is not thread-safe.
 64‚Üí * Make sure to apply proper synchronization if you are using a single instance from multiple threads.
 65‚Üí */
 66‚Üípublic class DateTimeComponents internal constructor(internal val contents: DateTimeComponentsContents = DateTimeComponentsContents()) {
 67‚Üí    public companion object {
 68‚Üí        /**
 69‚Üí         * Creates a [DateTimeFormat] for [DateTimeComponents] values using [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents].
 70‚Üí         *
 71‚Üí         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [DateTimeComponents.Formats].
 72‚Üí         */
 73‚Üí        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 74‚Üí        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> {
 75‚Üí            val builder = DateTimeComponentsFormat.Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
 76‚Üí            block(builder)
 77‚Üí            return DateTimeComponentsFormat(builder.build())
 78‚Üí        }
 79‚Üí    }
 80‚Üí
 81‚Üí    /**
 82‚Üí     * A collection of formats for parsing and formatting [DateTimeComponents] values.
 83‚Üí     *
 84‚Üí     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [DateTimeComponents.Format] to create a custom
 85‚Üí     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [DateTimeComponents] values.
 86‚Üí     */
 87‚Üí    public object Formats {
 88‚Üí
 89‚Üí        /**
 90‚Üí         * ISO 8601 extended format for dates and times with UTC offset.
 91‚Üí         *
 92‚Üí         * For specifying the time zone offset, the format uses the [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO] format, except that during
 93‚Üí         * parsing, specifying the minutes is optional.
 94‚Üí         *
 95‚Üí         * This format differs from [LocalTime.Formats.ISO] in its time part in that
 96‚Üí         * specifying the seconds is *not* optional.
 97‚Üí         *
 98‚Üí         * Examples of instants in the ISO 8601 format:
 99‚Üí         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00Z`
100‚Üí         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.50Z`
101‚Üí         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789Z`
102‚Üí         * - `2020-08-30T18:40:00+03:00`
103‚Üí         * - `2020-08-30T18:40:00+03:30:20`
104‚Üí         * * `2020-01-01T23:59:59.123456789+01`
105‚Üí         * * `+12020-01-31T23:59:59Z`
106‚Üí         *
107‚Üí         * This format uses the local date, local time, and UTC offset fields of [DateTimeComponents].
108‚Üí         *
109‚Üí         * See ISO-8601-1:2019, 5.4.2.1b), excluding the format without the offset.
110‚Üí         *
111‚Üí         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [Instant.toString] produces.
112‚Üí         */
113‚Üí        public val ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> = Format {
114‚Üí            date(ISO_DATE)
115‚Üí            alternativeParsing({
116‚Üí                char('t')
117‚Üí            }) {
118‚Üí                char('T')
119‚Üí            }
120‚Üí            hour()
121‚Üí            char(':')
122‚Üí            minute()
123‚Üí            char(':')
124‚Üí            second()
125‚Üí            optional {
126‚Üí                char('.')
127‚Üí                secondFraction(1, 9)
128‚Üí            }
129‚Üí            alternativeParsing({
130‚Üí                offsetHours()
131‚Üí            }) {
132‚Üí                offset(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)
133‚Üí            }
134‚Üí        }
135‚Üí
136‚Üí        /**
137‚Üí         * RFC 1123 format for dates and times with UTC offset.
138‚Üí         *
139‚Üí         * Examples of valid strings:
140‚Üí         * * `Mon, 30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 GMT`
141‚Üí         * * `Mon, 30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 -0300`

143‚Üí         *
144‚Üí         * North American and military time zone abbreviations are not supported.
145‚Üí         */

147‚Üí            alternativeParsing({
148‚Üí                // the day of week may be missing
149‚Üí            }) {
150‚Üí                dayOfWeek(DayOfWeekNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
151‚Üí                chars(", ")
152‚Üí            }
153‚Üí            dayOfMonth(Padding.NONE)
154‚Üí            char(' ')
155‚Üí            monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
156‚Üí            char(' ')
157‚Üí            year()
158‚Üí            char(' ')
159‚Üí            hour()
160‚Üí            char(':')
161‚Üí            minute()
162‚Üí            optional {
163‚Üí                char(':')
164‚Üí                second()
165‚Üí            }
166‚Üí            chars(" ")
167‚Üí            alternativeParsing({
168‚Üí                chars("UT")
169‚Üí            }, {
170‚Üí                chars("Z")
171‚Üí            }) {
172‚Üí                optional("GMT") {
173‚Üí                    offset(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)
174‚Üí                }
175‚Üí            }
176‚Üí        }
177‚Üí    }

493‚Üíinternal class DateTimeComponentsContents internal constructor(
494‚Üí    val date: IncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate(),
495‚Üí    val time: IncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime(),
496‚Üí    val offset: IncompleteUtcOffset = IncompleteUtcOffset(),
497‚Üí    var timeZoneId: String? = null,
498‚Üí) : DateFieldContainer by date, TimeFieldContainer by time, UtcOffsetFieldContainer by offset,
499‚Üí    DateTimeFieldContainer, Copyable<DateTimeComponentsContents> {
500‚Üí    override fun copy(): DateTimeComponentsContents =
501‚Üí        DateTimeComponentsContents(date.copy(), time.copy(), offset.copy(), timeZoneId)
502‚Üí
503‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
504‚Üí        other is DateTimeComponentsContents && other.date == date && other.time == time &&
505‚Üí            other.offset == offset && other.timeZoneId == timeZoneId
506‚Üí
507‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int =
508‚Üí        date.hashCode() xor time.hashCode() xor offset.hashCode() xor (timeZoneId?.hashCode() ?: 0)
509‚Üí}

524‚Üíinternal class DateTimeComponentsFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<DateTimeComponentsContents>) :
525‚Üí    AbstractDateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents, DateTimeComponentsContents>() {
526‚Üí    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: DateTimeComponents): DateTimeComponentsContents = value.contents
527‚Üí
528‚Üí    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: DateTimeComponentsContents): DateTimeComponents =
529‚Üí        DateTimeComponents(intermediate)
530‚Üí
531‚Üí    override val emptyIntermediate get() = emptyDateTimeComponentsContents
532‚Üí
533‚Üí    class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<DateTimeComponentsContents>) :
534‚Üí        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<DateTimeComponentsContents, Builder>, AbstractWithDateTimeBuilder,
535‚Üí        AbstractWithOffsetBuilder, DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents {
536‚Üí        override fun addFormatStructureForDateTime(structure: FormatStructure<DateTimeFieldContainer>) {
537‚Üí            actualBuilder.add(structure)

539‚Üí
540‚Üí        override fun addFormatStructureForOffset(structure: FormatStructure<UtcOffsetFieldContainer>) {
541‚Üí            actualBuilder.add(structure)

543‚Üí
544‚Üí        override fun timeZoneId() =

546‚Üí
547‚Üí        @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
548‚Üí        override fun dateTimeComponents(format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>) = when (format) {
549‚Üí            is DateTimeComponentsFormat -> actualBuilder.add(format.actualFormat)
550‚Üí        }
551‚Üí
552‚Üí        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
553‚Üí    }
554‚Üí}

556‚Üíprivate class TwoDigitNumber(private val reference: KMutableProperty0<Int?>) {
557‚Üí    operator fun getValue(thisRef: Any?, property: KProperty<*>) = reference.getValue(thisRef, property)
558‚Üí
559‚Üí    operator fun setValue(thisRef: Any?, property: KProperty<*>, value: Int?) {
560‚Üí        require(value === null || value in 0..99) { "${property.name} must be a two-digit number, got '$value'" }
561‚Üí        reference.setValue(thisRef, property, value)
562‚Üí    }
563‚Üí}
564‚Üí
565‚Üíprivate val emptyDateTimeComponentsContents = DateTimeComponentsContents()
566‚Üí

1‚Üí/*
  2‚Üí * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3‚Üí * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4‚Üí */
  5‚Üí
  6‚Üípackage kotlinx.datetime.internal.format
  7‚Üí
  8‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.DecimalFraction
  9‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.formatter.*
 10‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11‚Üí
 12‚Üí/**
 13‚Üí * A directive that specifies a way to parse and format the [field].
 14‚Üí */
 15‚Üíinternal interface FieldFormatDirective<in Target> {
 16‚Üí    /**
 17‚Üí     * The field parsed and formatted by this directive.
 18‚Üí     */
 19‚Üí    val field: FieldSpec<Target, *>
 20‚Üí
 21‚Üí    /**
 22‚Üí     * The formatter operation that formats the field.
 23‚Üí     */
 24‚Üí    fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target>
 25‚Üí
 26‚Üí    /**
 27‚Üí     * The parser structure that parses the field.
 28‚Üí     */
 29‚Üí    fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target>
 30‚Üí
 31‚Üí    /**
 32‚Üí     * The string with the code that, when evaluated in the builder context, appends the directive.
 33‚Üí     */
 34‚Üí    val builderRepresentation: String
 35‚Üí}
 36‚Üí
 37‚Üí/**
 38‚Üí * A directive for a decimal format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 39‚Üí * The field is formatted with the field padded to [minDigits] with zeroes,
 40‚Üí * and the parser expects the field to be at least [minDigits] digits long.
 41‚Üí */
 42‚Üíinternal abstract class UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 43‚Üí    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 44‚Üí    private val minDigits: Int,
 45‚Üí    private val spacePadding: Int?,
 46‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 47‚Üí
 48‚Üí    private val maxDigits: Int = field.maxDigits
 49‚Üí
 50‚Üí    init {
 51‚Üí        require(minDigits >= 0) {
 52‚Üí            "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative"
 53‚Üí        }
 54‚Üí        require(maxDigits >= minDigits) {
 55‚Üí            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 56‚Üí        }
 57‚Üí        if (spacePadding != null) {
 58‚Üí            require(spacePadding > minDigits) {
 59‚Üí                "The space padding ($spacePadding) should be more than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 60‚Üí            }
 61‚Üí        }
 62‚Üí    }
 63‚Üí
 64‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
 65‚Üí        val formatter = UnsignedIntFormatterStructure(
 66‚Üí            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
 67‚Üí            zeroPadding = minDigits,
 68‚Üí        )
 69‚Üí        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
 70‚Üí    }
 71‚Üí
 72‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
 73‚Üí        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, field.accessor, field.name)
 74‚Üí}
 75‚Üí
 76‚Üí/**
 77‚Üí * A directive for a string-based format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 78‚Üí */
 79‚Üíinternal abstract class NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 80‚Üí    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 81‚Üí    private val values: List<String>,
 82‚Üí    private val name: String,
 83‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 84‚Üí
 85‚Üí    init {
 86‚Üí        require(values.size == field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1) {
 87‚Üí            "The number of values (${values.size}) in $values does not match the range of the field (${field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1})"
 88‚Üí        }
 89‚Üí    }
 90‚Üí
 91‚Üí    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
 92‚Üí        values.getOrNull(it-field.minValue)
 93‚Üí            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
 94‚Üí    }
 95‚Üí
 96‚Üí    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
 97‚Üí        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
 98‚Üí            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, values.indexOf(newValue) + field.minValue)?.let {
 99‚Üí                values[it - field.minValue]
100‚Üí            }
101‚Üí
102‚Üí        override val name: String get() = this@NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective.name
103‚Üí    }
104‚Üí
105‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
106‚Üí        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
107‚Üí
108‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
109‚Üí        ParserStructure(
110‚Üí            listOf(
111‚Üí                StringSetParserOperation(values, AssignableString(), "One of $values for $name")
112‚Üí            ), emptyList()
113‚Üí        )
114‚Üí}
115‚Üí
116‚Üí/**
117‚Üí * A directive for a string-based format of an enum field.
118‚Üí */
119‚Üíinternal abstract class NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target, Type>(
120‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Type>,
121‚Üí    private val mapping: Map<Type, String>,
122‚Üí    private val name: String,
123‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
124‚Üí
125‚Üí    private val reverseMapping = mapping.entries.associate { it.value to it.key }
126‚Üí
127‚Üí    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
128‚Üí        mapping[field.accessor.getterNotNull(target)]
129‚Üí            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
130‚Üí    }
131‚Üí
132‚Üí    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
133‚Üí        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
134‚Üí            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, reverseMapping[newValue]!!)?.let { mapping[it] }
135‚Üí
136‚Üí        override val name: String get() = this@NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective.name
137‚Üí    }
138‚Üí
139‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
140‚Üí        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
141‚Üí
142‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
143‚Üí        ParserStructure(
144‚Üí            listOf(
145‚Üí                StringSetParserOperation(mapping.values, AssignableString(), "One of ${mapping.values} for $name")
146‚Üí            ), emptyList()
147‚Üí        )
148‚Üí}
149‚Üí
150‚Üíinternal abstract class StringFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
151‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, String>,
152‚Üí    private val acceptedStrings: Set<String>,
153‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
154‚Üí
155‚Üí    init {
156‚Üí        require(acceptedStrings.isNotEmpty()) {
157‚Üí            "The set of accepted strings is empty"
158‚Üí        }
159‚Üí    }
160‚Üí
161‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
162‚Üí        StringFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull)
163‚Üí
164‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
165‚Üí        ParserStructure(
166‚Üí            listOf(StringSetParserOperation(acceptedStrings, field.accessor, field.name)),
167‚Üí            emptyList()
168‚Üí        )
169‚Üí}
170‚Üí
171‚Üíinternal abstract class SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
172‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
173‚Üí    private val minDigits: Int?,
174‚Üí    private val maxDigits: Int?,
175‚Üí    private val spacePadding: Int?,
176‚Üí    private val outputPlusOnExceededWidth: Int?,
177‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
178‚Üí
179‚Üí    init {
180‚Üí        require(minDigits == null || minDigits >= 0) { "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative" }
181‚Üí        require(maxDigits == null || minDigits == null || maxDigits >= minDigits) {
182‚Üí            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
183‚Üí        }
184‚Üí    }
185‚Üí
186‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
187‚Üí        val formatter = SignedIntFormatterStructure(
188‚Üí            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
189‚Üí            zeroPadding = minDigits ?: 0,
190‚Üí            outputPlusOnExceededWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
191‚Üí        )
192‚Üí        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
193‚Üí    }
194‚Üí
195‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
196‚Üí        SignedIntParser(
197‚Üí            minDigits = minDigits,
198‚Üí            maxDigits = maxDigits,
199‚Üí            spacePadding = spacePadding,
200‚Üí            field.accessor,
201‚Üí            field.name,
202‚Üí            plusOnExceedsWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
203‚Üí        )
204‚Üí}
205‚Üí
206‚Üíinternal abstract class DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
207‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, DecimalFraction>,
208‚Üí    private val minDigits: Int,
209‚Üí    private val maxDigits: Int,
210‚Üí    private val zerosToAdd: List<Int>,
211‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
212‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
213‚Üí        DecimalFractionFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull, minDigits, maxDigits, zerosToAdd)
214‚Üí
215‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> = ParserStructure(
216‚Üí        listOf(
217‚Üí            NumberSpanParserOperation(
218‚Üí                listOf(FractionPartConsumer(minDigits, maxDigits, field.accessor, field.name))
219‚Üí            )
220‚Üí        ),
221‚Üí        emptyList()
222‚Üí    )
223‚Üí}
224‚Üí
225‚Üíinternal abstract class ReducedIntFieldDirective<in Target>(
226‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
227‚Üí    private val digits: Int,
228‚Üí    private val base: Int,
229‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
230‚Üí
231‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
232‚Üí        ReducedIntFormatterStructure(
233‚Üí            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
234‚Üí            digits = digits,
235‚Üí            base = base,
236‚Üí        )
237‚Üí
238‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
239‚Üí        ReducedIntParser(digits = digits, base = base, field.accessor, field.name)
240‚Üí}
241‚Üí
