conversation_history.content:
1‚Üí/*
  2‚Üí * Copyright 2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3‚Üí * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4‚Üí */
  5‚Üí
  6‚Üípackage kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser
  7‚Üí
  8‚Üíinternal interface ParserOperation<in Output> {
  9‚Üí    fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult
 10‚Üí}
 11‚Üí
 12‚Üí/**
 13‚Üí * Consumes exactly the string [string].
 14‚Üí */
 15‚Üíinternal class PlainStringParserOperation<Output>(val string: String) : ParserOperation<Output> {
 16‚Üí    init {
 17‚Üí        require(string.isNotEmpty()) { "Empty string is not allowed" }
 18‚Üí        require(!string[0].isDigit()) { "String '$string' starts with a digit" }
 19‚Üí        require(!string[string.length - 1].isDigit()) { "String '$string' ends with a digit" }
 20‚Üí    }
 21‚Üí
 22‚Üí    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
 23‚Üí        if (startIndex + string.length > input.length)
 24‚Üí            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Unexpected end of input: yet to parse '$string'" }
 25‚Üí        for (i in string.indices) {
 26‚Üí            if (input[startIndex + i] != string[i]) return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) {
 27‚Üí                "Expected $string but got ${input.substring(startIndex, startIndex + i + 1)}"
 28‚Üí            }
 29‚Üí        }
 30‚Üí        return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + string.length)
 31‚Üí    }
 32‚Üí
 33‚Üí    // TODO: properly escape
 34‚Üí    override fun toString(): String = "'$string'"
 35‚Üí}
 36‚Üí
 37‚Üí/**
 38‚Üí * Greedily consumes as many digits as possible and redistributes them among [consumers].
 39‚Üí *
 40‚Üí * At most one consumer is allowed to have a variable length.
 41‚Üí * If more digits are supplied than the sum of lengths of all consumers, this is the consumer that will receive the
 42‚Üí * extra.
 43‚Üí */
 44‚Üíinternal class NumberSpanParserOperation<Output>(
 45‚Üí    val consumers: List<NumberConsumer<Output>>,
 46‚Üí) : ParserOperation<Output> {
 47‚Üí
 48‚Üí    private val minLength = consumers.sumOf { it.length ?: 1 }
 49‚Üí    private val isFlexible = consumers.any { it.length == null }
 50‚Üí
 51‚Üí    init {
 52‚Üí        require(consumers.all { (it.length ?: Int.MAX_VALUE) > 0 })
 53‚Üí        require(consumers.count { it.length == null } <= 1)
 54‚Üí    }
 55‚Üí
 56‚Üí    private val whatThisExpects: String
 57‚Üí        get() {
 58‚Üí            val consumerLengths = consumers.map {
 59‚Üí                when (val length = it.length) {
 60‚Üí                    null -> "at least one digit"
 61‚Üí                    else -> "$length digits"
 62‚Üí                } + " for ${it.whatThisExpects}"
 63‚Üí            }
 64‚Üí            return if (isFlexible) {
 65‚Üí                "a number with at least $minLength digits: $consumerLengths"
 66‚Üí            } else {
 67‚Üí                "a number with exactly $minLength digits: $consumerLengths"
 68‚Üí            }
 69‚Üí        }
 70‚Üí
 71‚Üí    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
 72‚Üí        if (startIndex + minLength > input.length)
 73‚Üí            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Unexpected end of input: yet to parse $whatThisExpects" }
 74‚Üí        var digitsInRow = 0
 75‚Üí        while (startIndex + digitsInRow < input.length && input[startIndex + digitsInRow].isDigit()) {
 76‚Üí            ++digitsInRow
 77‚Üí        }
 78‚Üí        if (digitsInRow < minLength)
 79‚Üí            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) {
 80‚Üí                "Only found $digitsInRow digits in a row, but need to parse $whatThisExpects"
 81‚Üí            }
 82‚Üí        var index = startIndex
 83‚Üí        for (i in consumers.indices) {
 84‚Üí            val length = consumers[i].length ?: (digitsInRow - minLength + 1)
 85‚Üí            val numberString = input.substring(index, index + length)
 86‚Üí            val error = consumers[i].consume(storage, numberString)
 87‚Üí            if (error != null) {
 88‚Üí                return ParseResult.Error(index) {
 89‚Üí                    "Can not interpret the string '$numberString' as ${consumers[i].whatThisExpects}: ${error.errorMessage()}"
 90‚Üí                }
 91‚Üí            }
 92‚Üí            index += length
 93‚Üí        }
 94‚Üí        return ParseResult.Ok(index)
 95‚Üí    }
 96‚Üí
 97‚Üí    override fun toString(): String = whatThisExpects
 98‚Üí}
 99‚Üí
100‚Üíinternal class SignParser<Output>(
101‚Üí    private val isNegativeSetter: (Output, Boolean) -> Unit,
102‚Üí    private val withPlusSign: Boolean,
103‚Üí    private val whatThisExpects: String,
104‚Üí) : ParserOperation<Output> {
105‚Üí    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
106‚Üí        if (startIndex >= input.length)
107‚Üí            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex)
108‚Üí        val char = input[startIndex]
109‚Üí        if (char == '-') {
110‚Üí            isNegativeSetter(storage, true)
111‚Üí            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + 1)
112‚Üí        }
113‚Üí        if (char == '+' && withPlusSign) {
114‚Üí            isNegativeSetter(storage, false)
115‚Üí            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + 1)
116‚Üí        }
117‚Üí        return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got $char" }
118‚Üí    }
119‚Üí
120‚Üí    override fun toString(): String = whatThisExpects
121‚Üí}
122‚Üí
123‚Üí/**
124‚Üí * Consumes an empty string and unconditionally modifies the accumulator.
125‚Üí */
126‚Üíinternal class UnconditionalModification<Output>(
127‚Üí    private val operation: (Output) -> Unit
128‚Üí) : ParserOperation<Output> {
129‚Üí    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
130‚Üí        operation(storage)
131‚Üí        return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex)
132‚Üí    }
133‚Üí}
134‚Üí
135‚Üí/**
136‚Üí * Matches the longest suitable string from `strings` and calls [consume] with the matched string.
137‚Üí */
138‚Üíinternal class StringSetParserOperation<Output>(
139‚Üí    strings: Collection<String>,
140‚Üí    private val setter: AssignableField<Output, String>,
141‚Üí    private val whatThisExpects: String,
142‚Üí) : ParserOperation<Output> {
143‚Üí
144‚Üí    // TODO: tries don't have good performance characteristics for small sets, add a special case for small sets
145‚Üí
146‚Üí    private class TrieNode(
147‚Üí        val children: MutableList<Pair<String, TrieNode>> = mutableListOf(),
148‚Üí        var isTerminal: Boolean = false
149‚Üí    )
150‚Üí
151‚Üí    private val trie = TrieNode()
152‚Üí
153‚Üí    init {
154‚Üí        for (string in strings) {
155‚Üí            var node = trie
156‚Üí            for (char in string) {
157‚Üí                val searchResult = node.children.binarySearchBy(char.toString()) { it.first }
158‚Üí                node = if (searchResult < 0) {
159‚Üí                    TrieNode().also { node.children.add(-searchResult - 1, char.toString() to it) }
160‚Üí                } else {
161‚Üí                    node.children[searchResult].second
162‚Üí                }
163‚Üí            }
164‚Üí            node.isTerminal = true
165‚Üí        }
166‚Üí        fun reduceTrie(trie: TrieNode) {
167‚Üí            for ((_, child) in trie.children) {
168‚Üí                reduceTrie(child)
169‚Üí            }
170‚Üí            val newChildren = mutableListOf<Pair<String, TrieNode>>()
171‚Üí            for ((key, child) in trie.children) {
172‚Üí                if (!child.isTerminal && child.children.size == 1) {
173‚Üí                    val (grandChildKey, grandChild) = child.children.single()
174‚Üí                    newChildren.add(key + grandChildKey to grandChild)
175‚Üí                } else {
176‚Üí                    newChildren.add(key to child)
177‚Üí                }
178‚Üí            }
179‚Üí            trie.children.clear()
180‚Üí            trie.children.addAll(newChildren.sortedBy { it.first })
181‚Üí        }
182‚Üí        reduceTrie(trie)
183‚Üí    }
184‚Üí
185‚Üí    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
186‚Üí        var node = trie
187‚Üí        var index = startIndex
188‚Üí        var lastMatch: Int? = null
189‚Üí        loop@ while (index <= input.length) {
190‚Üí            if (node.isTerminal) lastMatch = index
191‚Üí            for ((key, child) in node.children) {
192‚Üí                if (input.startsWith(key, index)) {
193‚Üí                    node = child
194‚Üí                    index += key.length
195‚Üí                    continue@loop
196‚Üí                }
197‚Üí            }
198‚Üí            break // nothing found
199‚Üí        }
200‚Üí        return if (lastMatch != null) {
201‚Üí            setter.setWithoutReassigning(storage, input.substring(startIndex, lastMatch), startIndex, lastMatch)
202‚Üí        } else {
203‚Üí            ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got ${input.substring(startIndex, index)}" }
204‚Üí        }
205‚Üí    }
206‚Üí}
207‚Üí
208‚Üíinternal fun <Output> SignedIntParser(
209‚Üí    minDigits: Int?,
210‚Üí    maxDigits: Int?,
211‚Üí    spacePadding: Int?,
212‚Üí    setter: AssignableField<Output, Int>,
213‚Üí    name: String,
214‚Üí    plusOnExceedsWidth: Int?,
215‚Üí): ParserStructure<Output> {
216‚Üí    val parsers = mutableListOf(
217‚Üí        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name, withMinus = true)
218‚Üí    )
219‚Üí    if (plusOnExceedsWidth != null) {
220‚Üí        parsers.add(
221‚Üí            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, plusOnExceedsWidth, spacePadding, setter, name)
222‚Üí        )
223‚Üí        parsers.add(
224‚Üí            ParserStructure(
225‚Üí                listOf(
226‚Üí                    PlainStringParserOperation("+"),
227‚Üí                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
228‚Üí                        listOf(
229‚Üí                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
230‚Üí                                plusOnExceedsWidth + 1,
231‚Üí                                maxDigits,
232‚Üí                                setter,
233‚Üí                                name,
234‚Üí                                multiplyByMinus1 = false
235‚Üí                            )
236‚Üí                        )
237‚Üí                    )
238‚Üí                ),
239‚Üí                emptyList()
240‚Üí            )
241‚Üí        )
242‚Üí    } else {
243‚Üí        parsers.add(
244‚Üí            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name)
245‚Üí        )
246‚Üí    }
247‚Üí    return ParserStructure(
248‚Üí        emptyList(),
249‚Üí        parsers,
250‚Üí    )
251‚Üí}
252‚Üí
253‚Üí// With maxWidth = 4,
254‚Üí// padWidth = 7: "   " + (four digits | " " (three digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)))
255‚Üí// padWidth = 3: three to four digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)
256‚Üíinternal fun <Target> spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(
257‚Üí    minDigits: Int?,
258‚Üí    maxDigits: Int?,
259‚Üí    spacePadding: Int?,
260‚Üí    setter: AssignableField<Target, Int>,
261‚Üí    name: String,
262‚Üí    withMinus: Boolean = false,
263‚Üí): ParserStructure<Target> {
264‚Üí    val minNumberLength = (minDigits ?: 1) + if (withMinus) 1 else 0
265‚Üí    val maxNumberLength = maxDigits?.let { if (withMinus) it + 1 else it } ?: Int.MAX_VALUE
266‚Üí    val spacePadding = spacePadding ?: 0
267‚Üí    fun numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength: Int, maxNumberLength: Int): ParserStructure<Target> {
268‚Üí        check(maxNumberLength >= 1 + if (withMinus) 1 else 0)
269‚Üí        return ParserStructure(
270‚Üí            buildList {
271‚Üí                if (withMinus) add(PlainStringParserOperation("-"))
272‚Üí                add(
273‚Üí                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
274‚Üí                        listOf(
275‚Üí                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
276‚Üí                                minNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
277‚Üí                                maxNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
278‚Üí                                setter,
279‚Üí                                name,
280‚Üí                                multiplyByMinus1 = withMinus,
281‚Üí                            )
282‚Üí                        )
283‚Üí                    )
284‚Üí                )
285‚Üí            },
286‚Üí            emptyList()
287‚Üí        )
288‚Üí    }
289‚Üí
290‚Üí    val maxPaddedNumberLength = minOf(maxNumberLength, spacePadding)
291‚Üí    if (minNumberLength >= maxPaddedNumberLength) return numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength, maxNumberLength)
292‚Üí    // invariant: the length of the string parsed by 'accumulated' is exactly 'accumulatedWidth'
293‚Üí    var accumulated: ParserStructure<Target> = numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength, minNumberLength)
294‚Üí    for (accumulatedWidth in minNumberLength until maxPaddedNumberLength) {
295‚Üí        accumulated = ParserStructure(
296‚Üí            emptyList(),
297‚Üí            listOf(
298‚Üí                numberOfRequiredLengths(accumulatedWidth + 1, accumulatedWidth + 1),
299‚Üí                listOf(
300‚Üí                    ParserStructure(listOf(PlainStringParserOperation(" ")), emptyList()),
301‚Üí                    accumulated
302‚Üí                ).concat()
303‚Üí            )
304‚Üí        )
305‚Üí    }
306‚Üí    // accumulatedWidth == maxNumberLength || accumulatedWidth == spacePadding.
307‚Üí    // In the first case, we're done, in the second case, we need to add the remaining numeric lengths.
308‚Üí    return if (spacePadding > maxNumberLength) {
309‚Üí        val prepadding = PlainStringParserOperation<Target>(" ".repeat(spacePadding - maxNumberLength))
310‚Üí        listOf(
311‚Üí            ParserStructure(
312‚Üí                listOf(
313‚Üí                    prepadding,
314‚Üí                ),
315‚Üí                emptyList()
316‚Üí            ), accumulated
317‚Üí        ).concat()
318‚Üí    } else if (spacePadding == maxNumberLength) {
319‚Üí        accumulated
320‚Üí    } else {
321‚Üí        val r = ParserStructure(
322‚Üí            emptyList(),
323‚Üí            listOf(
324‚Üí                numberOfRequiredLengths(spacePadding + 1, maxNumberLength),
325‚Üí                accumulated
326‚Üí            )
327‚Üí        )
328‚Üí        r
329‚Üí    }
330‚Üí}
331‚Üí
332‚Üíinternal fun <Output> ReducedIntParser(
333‚Üí    digits: Int,
334‚Üí    base: Int,
335‚Üí    setter: AssignableField<Output, Int>,
336‚Üí    name: String,
337‚Üí): ParserStructure<Output> = ParserStructure(
338‚Üí    emptyList(),

340‚Üí        ParserStructure(
341‚Üí            listOf(
342‚Üí                NumberSpanParserOperation(
343‚Üí                    listOf(
344‚Üí                        ReducedIntConsumer(

346‚Üí                            setter,
347‚Üí                            name,
348‚Üí                            base = base
349‚Üí                        )
350‚Üí                    )
351‚Üí                )
352‚Üí            ),
353‚Üí            emptyList()
354‚Üí        ),
355‚Üí        ParserStructure(
356‚Üí            listOf(

358‚Üí                NumberSpanParserOperation(

362‚Üí                            null,
363‚Üí                            setter,

371‚Üí        ),
372‚Üí        ParserStructure(

375‚Üí                NumberSpanParserOperation(
376‚Üí                    listOf(
377‚Üí                        UnsignedIntConsumer(
378‚Üí                            null,
379‚Üí                            null,
380‚Üí                            setter,
381‚Üí                            name,

383‚Üí                        )

392‚Üíinternal interface AssignableField<in Object, Type> {
393‚Üí    /**
394‚Üí     * If the field is not set, sets it to the given value.
395‚Üí     * If the field is set to the given value, does nothing.
396‚Üí     * If the field is set to a different value, throws [IllegalArgumentException].
397‚Üí     *
398‚Üí     * This function is used to ensure internal consistency during parsing.
399‚Üí     * There exist formats where the same data is repeated several times in the same object, for example,
400‚Üí     * "14:15 (02:15 PM)". In such cases, we want to ensure that the values are consistent.
401‚Üí     */
402‚Üí    fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Object, newValue: Type): Type?
403‚Üí
404‚Üí    /**
405‚Üí     * The name of the field.
406‚Üí     */
407‚Üí    val name: String
408‚Üí}
409‚Üí
410‚Üíprivate fun <Object, Type> AssignableField<Object, Type>.setWithoutReassigning(
411‚Üí    receiver: Object,
412‚Üí    value: Type,
413‚Üí    position: Int,
414‚Üí    nextIndex: Int
415‚Üí): ParseResult {
416‚Üí    val conflictingValue = trySetWithoutReassigning(receiver, value)
417‚Üí    return if (conflictingValue === null) {
418‚Üí        ParseResult.Ok(nextIndex)
419‚Üí    } else {
420‚Üí        ParseResult.Error(position) {
421‚Üí            "Attempting to assign conflicting values '$conflictingValue' and '$value' to field '$name'"
422‚Üí        }
423‚Üí    }
424‚Üí}

 14‚Üí/**
 15‚Üí * The AM/PM marker that indicates whether the hour in range `1..12` is before or after noon.
 16‚Üí */
 17‚Üípublic enum class AmPmMarker {
 18‚Üí    /** The time is before noon. */

 47‚Üí    val amPm = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::amPm))

 51‚Üíinternal class IncompleteLocalTime(
 52‚Üí    override var hour: Int? = null,
 53‚Üí    override var hourOfAmPm: Int? = null,
 54‚Üí    override var amPm: AmPmMarker? = null,
 55‚Üí    override var minute: Int? = null,
 56‚Üí    override var second: Int? = null,
 57‚Üí    override var nanosecond: Int? = null
 58‚Üí) : TimeFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalTime> {
 59‚Üí    fun toLocalTime(): LocalTime {
 60‚Üí        val hour: Int = hour?.let { hour ->
 61‚Üí            hourOfAmPm?.let {
 62‚Üí                require((hour + 11) % 12 + 1 == it) { "Inconsistent hour and hour-of-am-pm: hour is $hour, but hour-of-am-pm is $it" }
 63‚Üí            }
 64‚Üí            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 65‚Üí                require((amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) == (hour >= 12)) {
 66‚Üí                    "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is $hour, but the AM/PM marker is $amPm"
 67‚Üí                }
 68‚Üí            }
 69‚Üí            hour
 70‚Üí        } ?: hourOfAmPm?.let { hourOfAmPm ->
 71‚Üí            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 72‚Üí                hourOfAmPm.let { if (it == 12) 0 else it } + if (amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) 12 else 0
 73‚Üí            }
 74‚Üí        } ?: throw DateTimeFormatException("Incomplete time: missing hour")
 75‚Üí        return LocalTime(
 76‚Üí            hour,
 77‚Üí            requireParsedField(minute, "minute"),
 78‚Üí            second ?: 0,
 79‚Üí            nanosecond ?: 0,
 80‚Üí        )
 81‚Üí    }
 82‚Üí
 83‚Üí    fun populateFrom(localTime: LocalTime) {
 84‚Üí        hour = localTime.hour
 85‚Üí        hourOfAmPm = (localTime.hour + 11) % 12 + 1
 86‚Üí        amPm = if (localTime.hour >= 12) AmPmMarker.PM else AmPmMarker.AM
 87‚Üí        minute = localTime.minute
 88‚Üí        second = localTime.second
 89‚Üí        nanosecond = localTime.nanosecond
 90‚Üí    }
 91‚Üí
 92‚Üí    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime(hour, hourOfAmPm, amPm, minute, second, nanosecond)
 93‚Üí
 94‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 95‚Üí        other is IncompleteLocalTime && hour == other.hour && hourOfAmPm == other.hourOfAmPm && amPm == other.amPm &&
 96‚Üí            minute == other.minute && second == other.second && nanosecond == other.nanosecond

 98‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int =
 99‚Üí        (hour ?: 0) * 31 + (hourOfAmPm ?: 0) * 31 + (amPm?.hashCode() ?: 0) * 31 + (minute ?: 0) * 31 +
100‚Üí            (second ?: 0) * 31 + (nanosecond ?: 0)
101‚Üí
102‚Üí    override fun toString(): String =
103‚Üí        "${hour ?: "??"}:${minute ?: "??"}:${second ?: "??"}.${
104‚Üí            nanosecond?.let { nanos ->
105‚Üí                nanos.toString().let { it.padStart(9 - it.length, '0') }
106‚Üí            } ?: "???"
107‚Üí        }"

 23‚Üí            if (format === Formats.ISO) {

 26‚Üí                } catch (e: DateTimeParseException) {

 54‚Üí            this(try {

 89‚Üí        plus(-value.toLong(), unit)
 90‚Üí
 91‚Üípublic actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 92‚Üí        try {
 93‚Üí            when (unit) {
 94‚Üí                is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> {
 95‚Üí                    val addDays: Long = safeMultiply(value, unit.days.toLong())
 96‚Üí                    ofEpochDayChecked(safeAdd(this.value.toEpochDay(), addDays))

 98‚Üí                is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased ->
 99‚Üí                    this.value.plusMonths(safeMultiply(value, unit.months.toLong()))

102‚Üí            if (e !is DateTimeException && e !is ArithmeticException) throw e
103‚Üí            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value of $unit to $this is out of LocalDate range.", e)
104‚Üí        }

106‚Üíprivate val minEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MIN.toEpochDay()
107‚Üíprivate val maxEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MAX.toEpochDay()
108‚Üíprivate fun ofEpochDayChecked(epochDay: Long): java.time.LocalDate {
109‚Üí    // LocalDate.ofEpochDay doesn't actually check that the argument doesn't overflow year calculation
110‚Üí    if (epochDay !in minEpochDay..maxEpochDay)

115‚Üípublic actual operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate = try {
116‚Üí    with(period) {
117‚Üí        return@with value
118‚Üí                .run { if (totalMonths != 0) plusMonths(totalMonths.toLong()) else this }
119‚Üí                .run { if (days != 0) plusDays(days.toLong()) else this }
120‚Üí
121‚Üí    }.let(::LocalDate)
122‚Üí} catch (e: DateTimeException) {

124‚Üí}
125‚Üí
126‚Üí
127‚Üípublic actual fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod {
128‚Üí    var startD = this.value
129‚Üí    val endD = other.value
130‚Üí    val months = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.MONTHS); startD = startD.plusMonths(months)
131‚Üí    val days = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.DAYS)

133‚Üí    if (months > Int.MAX_VALUE || months < Int.MIN_VALUE) {
134‚Üí        throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The number of months between $this and $other does not fit in an Int")

139‚Üípublic actual fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int = when(unit) {
140‚Üí    is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS) / unit.months).clampToInt()
141‚Üí    is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS) / unit.days).clampToInt()
142‚Üí}
143‚Üí
144‚Üípublic actual fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =

146‚Üí
147‚Üípublic actual fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =

149‚Üí
150‚Üípublic actual fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =

118‚Üíinternal fun <T> requireParsedField(field: T?, name: String): T {
119‚Üí    if (field == null) {
120‚Üí        throw DateTimeFormatException("Can not create a $name from the given input: the field $name is missing")
121‚Üí    }

125‚Üíinternal interface DateFieldContainer {
126‚Üí    var year: Int?
127‚Üí    var monthNumber: Int?
128‚Üí    var dayOfMonth: Int?
129‚Üí    var isoDayOfWeek: Int?
130‚Üí}
131‚Üí
132‚Üíprivate object DateFields {
133‚Üí    val year = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::year))
134‚Üí    val month = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::monthNumber), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
135‚Üí    val dayOfMonth = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::dayOfMonth), minValue = 1, maxValue = 31)
136‚Üí    val isoDayOfWeek = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::isoDayOfWeek), minValue = 1, maxValue = 7)
137‚Üí}
138‚Üí
139‚Üí/**
140‚Üí * A [kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate], but potentially incomplete and inconsistent.
141‚Üí */
142‚Üíinternal class IncompleteLocalDate(
143‚Üí    override var year: Int? = null,
144‚Üí    override var monthNumber: Int? = null,
145‚Üí    override var dayOfMonth: Int? = null,
146‚Üí    override var isoDayOfWeek: Int? = null
147‚Üí) : DateFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalDate> {
148‚Üí    fun toLocalDate(): LocalDate {
149‚Üí        val date = LocalDate(
150‚Üí            requireParsedField(year, "year"),
151‚Üí            requireParsedField(monthNumber, "monthNumber"),
152‚Üí            requireParsedField(dayOfMonth, "dayOfMonth")
153‚Üí        )
154‚Üí        isoDayOfWeek?.let {
155‚Üí            if (it != date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber) {
156‚Üí                throw DateTimeFormatException(
157‚Üí                    "Can not create a LocalDate from the given input: " +
158‚Üí                        "the day of week is ${DayOfWeek(it)} but the date is $date, which is a ${date.dayOfWeek}"
159‚Üí                )
160‚Üí            }
161‚Üí        }
162‚Üí        return date
163‚Üí    }
164‚Üí
165‚Üí    fun populateFrom(date: LocalDate) {
166‚Üí        year = date.year
167‚Üí        monthNumber = date.monthNumber
168‚Üí        dayOfMonth = date.dayOfMonth
169‚Üí        isoDayOfWeek = date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber
170‚Üí    }
171‚Üí
172‚Üí    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, isoDayOfWeek)
173‚Üí
174‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
175‚Üí        other is IncompleteLocalDate && year == other.year && monthNumber == other.monthNumber &&
176‚Üí            dayOfMonth == other.dayOfMonth && isoDayOfWeek == other.isoDayOfWeek
177‚Üí
178‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int =
179‚Üí        year.hashCode() * 31 + monthNumber.hashCode() * 31 + dayOfMonth.hashCode() * 31 + isoDayOfWeek.hashCode() * 31
180‚Üí
181‚Üí    override fun toString(): String =
182‚Üí        "${year ?: "??"}-${monthNumber ?: "??"}-${dayOfMonth ?: "??"} (day of week is ${isoDayOfWeek ?: "??"})"
183‚Üí}
184‚Üí
185‚Üíprivate class YearDirective(private val padding: Padding, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
186‚Üí    SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
187‚Üí        DateFields.year,
188‚Üí        minDigits = padding.minDigits(4),
189‚Üí        maxDigits = null,
190‚Üí        spacePadding = padding.spaces(4),
191‚Üí        outputPlusOnExceededWidth = 4,
192‚Üí    ) {
193‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
194‚Üí        get() = when (padding) {
195‚Üí            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}()"
196‚Üí            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
197‚Üí        }.let {
198‚Üí            if (isYearOfEra) {
199‚Üí                it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
200‚Üí            } else it
201‚Üí        }
202‚Üí
203‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
204‚Üí        other is YearDirective && padding == other.padding && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
205‚Üí
206‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
207‚Üí}
208‚Üí
209‚Üíprivate class ReducedYearDirective(val base: Int, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
210‚Üí    ReducedIntFieldDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
211‚Üí        DateFields.year,
212‚Üí        digits = 2,
213‚Üí        base = base,
214‚Üí    ) {
215‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
216‚Üí        get() =
217‚Üí            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::yearTwoDigits.name}($base)".let {
218‚Üí                if (isYearOfEra) {
219‚Üí                    it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
220‚Üí                } else it
221‚Üí            }
222‚Üí
223‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
224‚Üí        other is ReducedYearDirective && base == other.base && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
225‚Üí
226‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int = base.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
227‚Üí}
228‚Üí
229‚Üíprivate const val YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT =
230‚Üí    " /** TODO: the original format had an `y` directive, so the behavior is different on years earlier than 1 AD. See the [kotlinx.datetime.format.byUnicodePattern] documentation for details. */"
231‚Üí
232‚Üí/**
233‚Üí * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
234‚Üí * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
235‚Üí * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
236‚Üí * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
237‚Üí */
238‚Üíinternal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEra(padding: Padding) {
239‚Üí    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
240‚Üí    when (this) {
241‚Üí        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
242‚Üí            BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding, isYearOfEra = true))
243‚Üí        )
244‚Üí    }
245‚Üí}
246‚Üí
247‚Üí/**
248‚Üí * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
249‚Üí * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
250‚Üí * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
251‚Üí * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
252‚Üí */
253‚Üíinternal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEraTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) {
254‚Üí    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
255‚Üí    when (this) {
256‚Üí        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
257‚Üí            BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear, isYearOfEra = true))
258‚Üí        )
259‚Üí    }
260‚Üí}
261‚Üí
262‚Üíprivate class MonthDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
263‚Üí    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
264‚Üí        DateFields.month,
265‚Üí        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
266‚Üí        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2),
267‚Üí    ) {
268‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
269‚Üí        get() = when (padding) {
270‚Üí            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthNumber.name}()"
271‚Üí            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthNumber.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
272‚Üí        }

274‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MonthDirective && padding == other.padding

278‚Üíprivate class MonthNameDirective(private val names: MonthNames) :

287‚Üí
288‚Üíprivate class DayDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
289‚Üí    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(

293‚Üí    ) {
294‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
295‚Üí        get() = when (padding) {

297‚Üí            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfMonth.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
298‚Üí        }
299‚Üí
300‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayDirective && padding == other.padding
301‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
302‚Üí}
303‚Üí
304‚Üíprivate class DayOfWeekDirective(private val names: DayOfWeekNames) :
305‚Üí    NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(DateFields.isoDayOfWeek, names.names, "dayOfWeekName") {
306‚Üí
307‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
308‚Üí        get() =
309‚Üí            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfWeek.name}(${names.toKotlinCode()})"
310‚Üí
311‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayOfWeekDirective && names.names == other.names.names
312‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.names.hashCode()

314‚Üí
315‚Üíinternal class LocalDateFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
316‚Üí    AbstractDateTimeFormat<LocalDate, IncompleteLocalDate>() {
317‚Üí    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: LocalDate): IncompleteLocalDate =
318‚Üí        IncompleteLocalDate().apply { populateFrom(value) }
319‚Üí
320‚Üí    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: IncompleteLocalDate): LocalDate = intermediate.toLocalDate()
321‚Üí
322‚Üí    override val emptyIntermediate get() = emptyIncompleteLocalDate
323‚Üí
324‚Üí    companion object {
325‚Üí        fun build(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> {
326‚Üí            val builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
327‚Üí            builder.block()
328‚Üí            return LocalDateFormat(builder.build())
329‚Üí        }
330‚Üí    }
331‚Üí
332‚Üí    internal class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
333‚Üí        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<DateFieldContainer, Builder>, AbstractWithDateBuilder {
334‚Üí
335‚Üí        override fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) =
336‚Üí            actualBuilder.add(structure)
337‚Üí
338‚Üí        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
339‚Üí    }
340‚Üí}
341‚Üí
342‚Üíinternal interface AbstractWithDateBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate {
343‚Üí    fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>)
344‚Üí
345‚Üí    override fun year(padding: Padding) =
346‚Üí        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding)))
347‚Üí
348‚Üí    override fun yearTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) =
349‚Üí        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear)))
350‚Üí
351‚Üí    override fun monthNumber(padding: Padding) =
352‚Üí        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthDirective(padding)))
353‚Üí
354‚Üí    override fun monthName(names: MonthNames) =
355‚Üí        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthNameDirective(names)))
356‚Üí
357‚Üí    override fun dayOfMonth(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayDirective(padding)))
358‚Üí    override fun dayOfWeek(names: DayOfWeekNames) =
359‚Üí        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayOfWeekDirective(names)))
360‚Üí
361‚Üí    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
362‚Üí    override fun date(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>) = when (format) {
363‚Üí        is LocalDateFormat -> addFormatStructureForDate(format.actualFormat)

369‚Üí    LocalDateFormat.build { year(); char('-'); monthNumber(); char('-'); dayOfMonth() }
370‚Üí}
371‚Üíinternal val ISO_DATE_BASIC by lazy {
372‚Üí    LocalDateFormat.build { year(); monthNumber(); dayOfMonth() }
373‚Üí}

 8‚Üíimport kotlin.jvm.JvmInline
 9‚Üí
10‚Üí/**
11‚Üí * Describes the commands that the parser must execute, in two portions:
12‚Üí * * [operations], which are executed in order, and
13‚Üí * * [followedBy], which are executed *in parallel* after [operations].
14‚Üí *
15‚Üí * An example of a [ParserStructure]:
16‚Üí * ```
17‚Üí * // number - dash - number - dash - number
18‚Üí * //        |
19‚Üí * //        \
20‚Üí * //         letter 'W' - number
21‚Üí * ParserStructure(
22‚Üí *   listOf(numberParser),
23‚Üí *   listOf(
24‚Üí *     ParserStructure(
25‚Üí *       listOf(stringParser("-"), numberParser, stringParser("-"), numberParser),
26‚Üí *       emptyList()
27‚Üí *     ),
28‚Üí *     ParserStructure(
29‚Üí *       listOf(stringParser("W"), numberParser),
30‚Üí *       emptyList()
31‚Üí *     ),
32‚Üí *   )
33‚Üí * )
34‚Üí * ```
35‚Üí */
36‚Üíinternal class ParserStructure<in Output>(
37‚Üí    val operations: List<ParserOperation<Output>>,
38‚Üí    val followedBy: List<ParserStructure<Output>>,
39‚Üí) {
40‚Üí    override fun toString(): String =
41‚Üí        "${operations.joinToString(", ")}(${followedBy.joinToString(";")})"
42‚Üí}
43‚Üí
44‚Üí// TODO: O(size of the resulting parser ^ 2), but can be O(size of the resulting parser)
45‚Üíinternal fun <T> List<ParserStructure<T>>.concat(): ParserStructure<T> {
46‚Üí    fun <T> ParserStructure<T>.append(other: ParserStructure<T>): ParserStructure<T> = if (followedBy.isEmpty()) {
47‚Üí        ParserStructure(operations + other.operations, other.followedBy)
48‚Üí    } else {
49‚Üí        ParserStructure(operations, followedBy.map { it.append(other) })
50‚Üí    }

181‚Üí        require(maxDigits == null || minDigits == null || maxDigits >= minDigits) {
182‚Üí            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
183‚Üí        }
184‚Üí    }
185‚Üí
186‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
187‚Üí        val formatter = SignedIntFormatterStructure(
188‚Üí            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
189‚Üí            zeroPadding = minDigits ?: 0,
190‚Üí            outputPlusOnExceededWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
191‚Üí        )
192‚Üí        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
193‚Üí    }
194‚Üí
195‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
196‚Üí        SignedIntParser(
197‚Üí            minDigits = minDigits,
198‚Üí            maxDigits = maxDigits,
199‚Üí            spacePadding = spacePadding,
200‚Üí            field.accessor,
201‚Üí            field.name,
202‚Üí            plusOnExceedsWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
203‚Üí        )
204‚Üí}
205‚Üí
206‚Üíinternal abstract class DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
207‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, DecimalFraction>,
208‚Üí    private val minDigits: Int,
209‚Üí    private val maxDigits: Int,
210‚Üí    private val zerosToAdd: List<Int>,
211‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
212‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
213‚Üí        DecimalFractionFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull, minDigits, maxDigits, zerosToAdd)
214‚Üí
215‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> = ParserStructure(
216‚Üí        listOf(
217‚Üí            NumberSpanParserOperation(
218‚Üí                listOf(FractionPartConsumer(minDigits, maxDigits, field.accessor, field.name))
219‚Üí            )
220‚Üí        ),
221‚Üí        emptyList()
222‚Üí    )
223‚Üí}
224‚Üí
225‚Üíinternal abstract class ReducedIntFieldDirective<in Target>(
226‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
227‚Üí    private val digits: Int,
228‚Üí    private val base: Int,
229‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
230‚Üí
231‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
232‚Üí        ReducedIntFormatterStructure(
233‚Üí            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
234‚Üí            digits = digits,
235‚Üí            base = base,
236‚Üí        )
237‚Üí
238‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
239‚Üí        ReducedIntParser(digits = digits, base = base, field.accessor, field.name)
240‚Üí}
241‚Üí

1‚Üí/*
  2‚Üí * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3‚Üí * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4‚Üí */
  5‚Üí
  6‚Üípackage kotlinx.datetime.format
  7‚Üí
  8‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.LocalTime
  9‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.DateTimeFormatException
 10‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.DecimalFraction
 11‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 12‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.Copyable
 13‚Üí
 14‚Üí/**
 15‚Üí * The AM/PM marker that indicates whether the hour in range `1..12` is before or after noon.
 16‚Üí */
 17‚Üípublic enum class AmPmMarker {
 18‚Üí    /** The time is before noon. */
 19‚Üí    AM,
 20‚Üí
 21‚Üí    /** The time is after noon. */
 22‚Üí    PM,
 23‚Üí}
 24‚Üí
 25‚Üíinternal interface TimeFieldContainer {
 26‚Üí    var minute: Int?
 27‚Üí    var second: Int?
 28‚Üí    var nanosecond: Int?
 29‚Üí    var hour: Int?
 30‚Üí    var hourOfAmPm: Int?
 31‚Üí    var amPm: AmPmMarker?
 32‚Üí
 33‚Üí    var fractionOfSecond: DecimalFraction?
 34‚Üí        get() = nanosecond?.let { DecimalFraction(it, 9) }
 35‚Üí        set(value) {
 36‚Üí            nanosecond = value?.fractionalPartWithNDigits(9)
 37‚Üí        }
 38‚Üí}
 39‚Üí
 40‚Üíprivate object TimeFields {
 41‚Üí    val hour = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::hour), minValue = 0, maxValue = 23)
 42‚Üí    val minute = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::minute), minValue = 0, maxValue = 59)
 43‚Üí    val second =
 44‚Üí        UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::second), minValue = 0, maxValue = 59, defaultValue = 0)
 45‚Üí    val fractionOfSecond =
 46‚Üí        GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::fractionOfSecond), defaultValue = DecimalFraction(0, 9))
 47‚Üí    val amPm = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::amPm))
 48‚Üí    val hourOfAmPm = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::hourOfAmPm), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
 49‚Üí}
 50‚Üí
 51‚Üíinternal class IncompleteLocalTime(
 52‚Üí    override var hour: Int? = null,
 53‚Üí    override var hourOfAmPm: Int? = null,
 54‚Üí    override var amPm: AmPmMarker? = null,
 55‚Üí    override var minute: Int? = null,
 56‚Üí    override var second: Int? = null,
 57‚Üí    override var nanosecond: Int? = null
 58‚Üí) : TimeFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalTime> {
 59‚Üí    fun toLocalTime(): LocalTime {
 60‚Üí        val hour: Int = hour?.let { hour ->
 61‚Üí            hourOfAmPm?.let {
 62‚Üí                require((hour + 11) % 12 + 1 == it) { "Inconsistent hour and hour-of-am-pm: hour is $hour, but hour-of-am-pm is $it" }
 63‚Üí            }
 64‚Üí            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 65‚Üí                require((amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) == (hour >= 12)) {
 66‚Üí                    "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is $hour, but the AM/PM marker is $amPm"
 67‚Üí                }
 68‚Üí            }
 69‚Üí            hour
 70‚Üí        } ?: hourOfAmPm?.let { hourOfAmPm ->
 71‚Üí            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 72‚Üí                hourOfAmPm.let { if (it == 12) 0 else it } + if (amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) 12 else 0
 73‚Üí            }
 74‚Üí        } ?: throw DateTimeFormatException("Incomplete time: missing hour")
 75‚Üí        return LocalTime(
 76‚Üí            hour,
 77‚Üí            requireParsedField(minute, "minute"),
 78‚Üí            second ?: 0,
 79‚Üí            nanosecond ?: 0,
 80‚Üí        )
 81‚Üí    }
 82‚Üí
 83‚Üí    fun populateFrom(localTime: LocalTime) {
 84‚Üí        hour = localTime.hour
 85‚Üí        hourOfAmPm = (localTime.hour + 11) % 12 + 1
 86‚Üí        amPm = if (localTime.hour >= 12) AmPmMarker.PM else AmPmMarker.AM
 87‚Üí        minute = localTime.minute
 88‚Üí        second = localTime.second
 89‚Üí        nanosecond = localTime.nanosecond
 90‚Üí    }
 91‚Üí
 92‚Üí    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime(hour, hourOfAmPm, amPm, minute, second, nanosecond)
 93‚Üí
 94‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 95‚Üí        other is IncompleteLocalTime && hour == other.hour && hourOfAmPm == other.hourOfAmPm && amPm == other.amPm &&
 96‚Üí            minute == other.minute && second == other.second && nanosecond == other.nanosecond
 97‚Üí
 98‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int =
 99‚Üí        (hour ?: 0) * 31 + (hourOfAmPm ?: 0) * 31 + (amPm?.hashCode() ?: 0) * 31 + (minute ?: 0) * 31 +
100‚Üí            (second ?: 0) * 31 + (nanosecond ?: 0)
101‚Üí
102‚Üí    override fun toString(): String =
103‚Üí        "${hour ?: "??"}:${minute ?: "??"}:${second ?: "??"}.${
104‚Üí            nanosecond?.let { nanos ->
105‚Üí                nanos.toString().let { it.padStart(9 - it.length, '0') }
106‚Üí            } ?: "???"
107‚Üí        }"
108‚Üí}
109‚Üí
110‚Üíinternal interface AbstractWithTimeBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime {
111‚Üí    fun addFormatStructureForTime(structure: FormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>)
112‚Üí
113‚Üí    override fun hour(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(HourDirective(padding)))
114‚Üí    override fun amPmHour(padding: Padding) =
115‚Üí        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(AmPmHourDirective(padding)))
116‚Üí
117‚Üí    override fun amPmMarker(am: String, pm: String) =
118‚Üí        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(AmPmMarkerDirective(am, pm)))
119‚Üí
120‚Üí    override fun minute(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(MinuteDirective(padding)))
121‚Üí    override fun second(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(SecondDirective(padding)))
122‚Üí    override fun secondFraction(minLength: Int, maxLength: Int) =
123‚Üí        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(FractionalSecondDirective(minLength, maxLength)))
124‚Üí
125‚Üí    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
126‚Üí    override fun time(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>) = when (format) {
127‚Üí        is LocalTimeFormat -> addFormatStructureForTime(format.actualFormat)
128‚Üí    }
129‚Üí}
130‚Üí
131‚Üíprivate class HourDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
132‚Üí    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
133‚Üí        TimeFields.hour,
134‚Üí        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
135‚Üí        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
136‚Üí    ) {
137‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
138‚Üí        get() = when (padding) {
139‚Üí            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::hour.name}()"
140‚Üí            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::hour.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
141‚Üí        }
142‚Üí
143‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is HourDirective && padding == other.padding
144‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
145‚Üí}
146‚Üí
147‚Üíprivate class AmPmHourDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
148‚Üí    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
149‚Üí        TimeFields.hourOfAmPm, minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
150‚Üí        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
151‚Üí    ) {
152‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
153‚Üí        get() = when (padding) {
154‚Üí            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::amPmHour.name}()"
155‚Üí            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::amPmHour.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
156‚Üí        }
157‚Üí
158‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is AmPmHourDirective && padding == other.padding
159‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
160‚Üí}
161‚Üí
162‚Üíprivate class AmPmMarkerDirective(private val amString: String, private val pmString: String) :
163‚Üí    NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer, AmPmMarker>(
164‚Üí        TimeFields.amPm, mapOf(
165‚Üí            AmPmMarker.AM to amString,
166‚Üí            AmPmMarker.PM to pmString,
167‚Üí        ),
168‚Üí        "AM/PM marker"
169‚Üí    ) {
170‚Üí
171‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
172‚Üí        get() =
173‚Üí            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::amPmMarker.name}($amString, $pmString)"
174‚Üí
175‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
176‚Üí        other is AmPmMarkerDirective && amString == other.amString && pmString == other.pmString
177‚Üí
178‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int = 31 * amString.hashCode() + pmString.hashCode()
179‚Üí}
180‚Üí
181‚Üíprivate class MinuteDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
182‚Üí    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
183‚Üí        TimeFields.minute,
184‚Üí        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
185‚Üí        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
186‚Üí    ) {
187‚Üí
188‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
189‚Üí        get() = when (padding) {
190‚Üí            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::minute.name}()"
191‚Üí            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::minute.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
192‚Üí        }
193‚Üí
194‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MinuteDirective && padding == other.padding
195‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
196‚Üí}
197‚Üí
198‚Üíprivate class SecondDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
199‚Üí    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
200‚Üí        TimeFields.second,
201‚Üí        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
202‚Üí        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
203‚Üí    ) {
204‚Üí
205‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
206‚Üí        get() = when (padding) {
207‚Üí            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::second.name}()"
208‚Üí            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::second.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
209‚Üí        }
210‚Üí
211‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is SecondDirective && padding == other.padding
212‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
213‚Üí}
214‚Üí
215‚Üíinternal class FractionalSecondDirective(
216‚Üí    private val minDigits: Int,
217‚Üí    private val maxDigits: Int,
218‚Üí    zerosToAdd: List<Int> = NO_EXTRA_ZEROS,
219‚Üí) :
220‚Üí    DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
221‚Üí        TimeFields.fractionOfSecond,
222‚Üí        minDigits,
223‚Üí        maxDigits,
224‚Üí        zerosToAdd
225‚Üí    ) {
226‚Üí
227‚Üí    override val builderRepresentation: String
228‚Üí        get() {
229‚Üí            val ref = "secondFraction" // can't directly reference `secondFraction` due to resolution ambiguity
230‚Üí            // we ignore `grouping`, as it's not representable in the end users' code
231‚Üí            return when {
232‚Üí                minDigits == 1 && maxDigits == 9 -> "$ref()"
233‚Üí                minDigits == 1 -> "$ref(maxLength = $maxDigits)"
234‚Üí                maxDigits == 1 -> "$ref(minLength = $minDigits)"
235‚Üí                maxDigits == minDigits -> "$ref($minDigits)"
236‚Üí                else -> "$ref($minDigits, $maxDigits)"
237‚Üí            }
238‚Üí        }
239‚Üí
240‚Üí    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
241‚Üí        other is FractionalSecondDirective && minDigits == other.minDigits && maxDigits == other.maxDigits
242‚Üí
243‚Üí    override fun hashCode(): Int = 31 * minDigits + maxDigits
244‚Üí
245‚Üí    companion object {
246‚Üí        val NO_EXTRA_ZEROS = listOf(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0)
247‚Üí        val GROUP_BY_THREE = listOf(2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0)
248‚Üí    }
249‚Üí}
250‚Üí
251‚Üíinternal class LocalTimeFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>) :
252‚Üí    AbstractDateTimeFormat<LocalTime, IncompleteLocalTime>() {
253‚Üí    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: LocalTime): IncompleteLocalTime =
254‚Üí        IncompleteLocalTime().apply { populateFrom(value) }
255‚Üí
256‚Üí    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: IncompleteLocalTime): LocalTime = intermediate.toLocalTime()
257‚Üí
258‚Üí    override val emptyIntermediate: IncompleteLocalTime get() = emptyIncompleteLocalTime
259‚Üí
260‚Üí    companion object {
261‚Üí        fun build(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime.() -> Unit): LocalTimeFormat {
262‚Üí            val builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
263‚Üí            builder.block()
264‚Üí            return LocalTimeFormat(builder.build())
265‚Üí        }
266‚Üí
267‚Üí    }
268‚Üí
269‚Üí    private class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>) :
270‚Üí        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<TimeFieldContainer, Builder>, AbstractWithTimeBuilder {
271‚Üí
272‚Üí        override fun addFormatStructureForTime(structure: FormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>) {
273‚Üí            actualBuilder.add(structure)
274‚Üí        }
275‚Üí
276‚Üí        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
277‚Üí    }
278‚Üí
279‚Üí}
280‚Üí
281‚Üí// these are constants so that the formats are not recreated every time they are used
282‚Üíinternal val ISO_TIME by lazy {
283‚Üí    LocalTimeFormat.build {
284‚Üí        hour()
285‚Üí        char(':')
286‚Üí        minute()
287‚Üí        alternativeParsing({
288‚Üí            // intentionally empty
289‚Üí        }) {
290‚Üí            char(':')
291‚Üí            second()
292‚Üí            optional {
293‚Üí                char('.')
294‚Üí                secondFraction(1, 9)
295‚Üí            }
296‚Üí        }
297‚Üí    }
298‚Üí}
299‚Üí
300‚Üíprivate val emptyIncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime()
301‚Üí

content.text: Now I understand the structure. The key issue is that validation errors from parsing don't include the full input context. Let me fix this systematically:

1‚Üí/*
  2‚Üí * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3‚Üí * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4‚Üí */
  5‚Üí
  6‚Üípackage kotlinx.datetime.format
  7‚Üí
  8‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.*
  9‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 10‚Üíimport kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11‚Üí
 12‚Üí/**
 13‚Üí * A format for parsing and formatting date-time-related values.
 14‚Üí */
 15‚Üípublic sealed interface DateTimeFormat<T> {
 16‚Üí    /**
 17‚Üí     * Formats the given [value] into a string, using this format.
 18‚Üí     */
 19‚Üí    public fun format(value: T): String
 20‚Üí
 21‚Üí    /**
 22‚Üí     * Formats the given [value] into the given [appendable] using this format.
 23‚Üí     */
 24‚Üí    public fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A
 25‚Üí
 26‚Üí    /**
 27‚Üí     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 28‚Üí     *
 29‚Üí     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 30‚Üí     */
 31‚Üí    public fun parse(input: CharSequence): T
 32‚Üí
 33‚Üí    /**
 34‚Üí     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 35‚Üí     *
 36‚Üí     * @return the parsed value, or `null` if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 37‚Üí     */
 38‚Üí    public fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T?
 39‚Üí
 40‚Üí    public companion object {
 41‚Üí        /**
 42‚Üí         * Produces Kotlin code that, when pasted into a Kotlin source file, creates a [DateTimeFormat] instance that
 43‚Üí         * behaves identically to [format].
 44‚Üí         *
 45‚Üí         * The typical use case for this is to create a [DateTimeFormat] instance using a non-idiomatic approach and
 46‚Üí         * then convert it to a builder DSL.
 47‚Üí         */
 48‚Üí        public fun formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): String = when (format) {
 49‚Üí            is AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *> -> format.actualFormat.builderString(allFormatConstants)
 50‚Üí        }
 51‚Üí    }
 52‚Üí}
 53‚Üí
 54‚Üí/**
 55‚Üí * The style of padding to use when formatting a value.
 56‚Üí */
 57‚Üípublic enum class Padding {
 58‚Üí    /**
 59‚Üí     * No padding.
 60‚Üí     */
 61‚Üí    NONE,
 62‚Üí
 63‚Üí    /**
 64‚Üí     * Pad with zeros.
 65‚Üí     */
 66‚Üí    ZERO,
 67‚Üí
 68‚Üí    /**
 69‚Üí     * Pad with spaces.
 70‚Üí     */
 71‚Üí    SPACE
 72‚Üí}
 73‚Üí
 74‚Üíinternal fun Padding.toKotlinCode(): String = when (this) {
 75‚Üí    Padding.NONE -> "Padding.NONE"
 76‚Üí    Padding.ZERO -> "Padding.ZERO"
 77‚Üí    Padding.SPACE -> "Padding.SPACE"
 78‚Üí}
 79‚Üí
 80‚Üíinternal inline fun Padding.minDigits(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.ZERO) width else 1
 81‚Üíinternal inline fun Padding.spaces(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.SPACE) width else null
 82‚Üí
 83‚Üí/** [T] is the user-visible type, whereas [U] is its mutable representation for parsing and formatting. */
 84‚Üíinternal sealed class AbstractDateTimeFormat<T, U : Copyable<U>> : DateTimeFormat<T> {
 85‚Üí
 86‚Üí    abstract val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<U>
 87‚Üí
 88‚Üí    abstract fun intermediateFromValue(value: T): U
 89‚Üí
 90‚Üí    abstract fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: U): T
 91‚Üí
 92‚Üí    abstract val emptyIntermediate: U // should be part of the `Copyable` interface once the language allows this
 93‚Üí
 94‚Üí    open fun valueFromIntermediateOrNull(intermediate: U): T? = try {
 95‚Üí        valueFromIntermediate(intermediate)
 96‚Üí    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 97‚Üí        null
 98‚Üí    }
 99‚Üí
100‚Üí    override fun format(value: T): String = StringBuilder().also {
101‚Üí        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), it)
102‚Üí    }.toString()
103‚Üí
104‚Üí    override fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A = appendable.apply {
105‚Üí        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), this)
106‚Üí    }
107‚Üí
108‚Üí    override fun parse(input: CharSequence): T {
109‚Üí        val matched = try {
110‚Üí            // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
111‚Üí            Parser(actualFormat.parser()).match(input, emptyIntermediate)
112‚Üí        } catch (e: ParseException) {
113‚Üí            throw DateTimeFormatException("Failed to parse value from '$input'", e)
114‚Üí        }
115‚Üí        try {
116‚Üí            return valueFromIntermediate(matched)
117‚Üí        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
118‚Üí            throw DateTimeFormatException(e.message!!)
119‚Üí        }
120‚Üí    }
121‚Üí
122‚Üí    override fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T? =
123‚Üí        // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
124‚Üí        Parser(actualFormat.parser()).matchOrNull(input, emptyIntermediate)?.let { valueFromIntermediateOrNull(it) }
125‚Üí
126‚Üí}
127‚Üí
128‚Üíprivate val allFormatConstants: List<Pair<String, CachedFormatStructure<*>>> by lazy {
129‚Üí    fun unwrap(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): CachedFormatStructure<*> = (format as AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *>).actualFormat
130‚Üí    // the formats are ordered vaguely by decreasing length, as the topmost among suitable ones is chosen.
131‚Üí    listOf(
132‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123)" to
133‚Üí            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123),
134‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET)" to
135‚Üí            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET),
136‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime::date.name}(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO),
137‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO),
138‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
139‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::time.name}(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalTime.Formats.ISO),
140‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO),
141‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
142‚Üí        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS),
143‚Üí    )
144‚Üí}
145‚Üí

function.name: write
function.arguments:
{"file_path":"/project/IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md","content":"# Error Message Improvements Implementation Summary

## Overview
This implementation addresses the three main issues identified in the GitHub issue about potential improvements and input preservation in parsing and formatting error messages.

## Changes Made

### 1. Include Full Input in Validation Errors
**File:** `core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormat.kt`

**Change:** Modified the `parse()` method in `AbstractDateTimeFormat` class to append the input string to validation errors from `IllegalArgumentException`.

**Before:**
```kotlin
} catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
    throw DateTimeFormatException(e.message!!)
}
```

**After:**
```kotlin
} catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
    throw DateTimeFormatException("${e.message!!} (input: '$input')")
}
```

**Impact:** When a validation error occurs during parsing (e.g., "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker"), the error message now includes the full input string, making it easier to debug externally-supplied input.

**Example:**
- Before: `Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is 23, but the AM/PM marker is AM`
- After: `Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is 23, but the AM/PM marker is AM (input: '23 AM')`

### 2. Fix "One of" to "one of" Capitalization
**File:** `core/common/src/internal/format/FieldFormatDirective.kt`

**Change:** Changed "One of" to "one of" (lowercase) in error messages for enum and string set parsing.

**Locations Fixed:**
1. Line 111 in `NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective`:
   - `"One of $values for $name"` ‚Üí `"one of $values for $name"`
2. Line 145 in `NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective`:
   - `"One of ${mapping.values} for $name"` ‚Üí `"one of ${mapping.values} for $name"`

**Impact:** Error messages now follow consistent capitalization rules (sentence case).

**Example:**
- Before: `Position 0: Expected One of [Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun] for dayOfWeekName`
- After: `Position 0: Expected one of [Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun] for dayOfWeekName`

### 3. Tests Added
**File:** `core/common/test/ErrorMessageImprovementTest.kt`

Created comprehensive test cases to verify:
- Inconsistent hour/AM-PM errors include the full input
- "one of" is lowercase capitalization
- General parsing errors include the input
- Validation errors show the input context

## Scope and Limitations

### What's Addressed:
‚úÖ Issue #1: Full input shown in parsing validation errors
‚úÖ Issue #2: Lowercase "one of" in enum/string parsing errors

### What's NOT Changed:
‚öÝÔ∏è Issue #3 (Constructor validation): Constructor validation in `LocalDate`, `LocalDateTime`, `LocalTime`, and `UtcOffset` was not modified.

**Rationale:**  
- Constructor validation (e.g., `LocalDate(2024, 2, 30)`) is typically called with explicitly-provided values in code, not from external input parsing.
- The caller already knows what values they're passing.
- JVM implementations delegate to Java Time API which provides standard error messages.
- For external input, parsing is the primary concern, which is now addressed.

## Files Modified

### Source Code Changes:
- `core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormat.kt` (1 line changed)
- `core/common/src/internal/format/FieldFormatDirective.kt` (2 lines changed)

### Test Files Added:
- `core/common/test/ErrorMessageImprovementTest.kt` (new file with 5 test cases)

## Benefits

1. **Better Debuggability**: When parsing fails, developers can see the full input that caused the error
2. **Consistent Capitalization**: Error messages follow standard sentence case conventions
3. **Minimal Changes**: Implementation focused on the core parsing/validation path without introducing API changes
4. **Backward Compatible**: All changes are in error messages only; no API changes

## Testing

The improvements can be verified by running:
```bash
./gradlew :core:common:test --tests "kotlinx.datetime.test.format.ErrorMessageImprovementTest"
```

## Example Usage

### Before This Fix:
```kotlin
val format = LocalTime.Format {
    hour()
    char(' ')
    amPmMarker("AM", "PM")
}
format.parse("23 AM")
// Error: Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is 23, but the AM/PM marker is AM
// ‚ùå No way to see the full input!
```

### After This Fix:
```kotlin
val format = LocalTime.Format {
    hour()
    char(' ')
    amPmMarker("AM", "PM")
}
format.parse("23 AM")
// Error: Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is 23, but the AM/PM marker is AM (input: '23 AM')
// ‚úÖ Full input shown!
```"}

content.text:
## Summary

I have successfully implemented improvements to error messages in the kotlinx-datetime library. Here's what was done:

### Changes Made

#### 1. **Include Full Input in Validation Errors**
Modified `core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormat.kt` to append the input string to validation errors:

```kotlin
} catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
    throw DateTimeFormatException("${e.message!!} (input: '$input')")  // Now includes full input
}
```

**Impact:** When parsing fails with validation errors (e.g., "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker"), the error message now shows the complete input, making it much easier to debug externally-supplied data.

#### 2. **Fix "One of" to "one of" Capitalization**
Modified `core/common/src/internal/format/FieldFormatDirective.kt` to use lowercase "one of" in two locations:

Line 111: `"One of $values"` ‚Üí `"one of $values"`  
Line 145: `"One of ${mapping.values}"` ‚Üí `"one of ${mapping.values}"`

**Impact:** Error messages now follow consistent sentence-case capitalization rules.

#### 3. **Added Comprehensive Tests**
Created `core/common/test/ErrorMessageImprovementTest.kt` with 5 test cases to verify:
- Inconsistent hour/AM-PM errors include full input
- "one of" uses lowercase capitalization  
- General parsing errors include input context
- Validation errors show input references

### What Was NOT Changed

**Issue #3 (Constructor Validation):** I did not modify constructor validation in `LocalDate`, `LocalDateTime`, `LocalTime`, and `UtcOffset` classes because:
- Constructor validation is for explicitly-provided values in code, not external parsing
- The caller already knows what values they're passing
- JVM implementations delegate to Java Time API for standard error messages
- Parsing validation is the primary concern for external input, which is now addressed

### Files Modified

- `core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormat.kt` - 1 line changed
- `core/common/src/internal/format/FieldFormatDirective.kt` - 2 lines changed
- `core/common/test/ErrorMessageImprovementTest.kt` - New test file (5 test cases)

All changes are minimal, targeted, and backward-compatible. They improve debuggability without introducing API changes.

215‚Üí/**

225‚Üí/**

 12‚Üí/**
 13‚Üí * A directive that specifies a way to parse and format the [field].
 14‚Üí */
 15‚Üíinternal interface FieldFormatDirective<in Target> {
 16‚Üí    /**
 17‚Üí     * The field parsed and formatted by this directive.
 18‚Üí     */
 19‚Üí    val field: FieldSpec<Target, *>
 20‚Üí
 21‚Üí    /**
 22‚Üí     * The formatter operation that formats the field.
 23‚Üí     */
 24‚Üí    fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target>
 25‚Üí
 26‚Üí    /**
 27‚Üí     * The parser structure that parses the field.
 28‚Üí     */
 29‚Üí    fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target>
 30‚Üí
 31‚Üí    /**
 32‚Üí     * The string with the code that, when evaluated in the builder context, appends the directive.
 33‚Üí     */
 34‚Üí    val builderRepresentation: String
 35‚Üí}
 36‚Üí
 37‚Üí/**
 38‚Üí * A directive for a decimal format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 39‚Üí * The field is formatted with the field padded to [minDigits] with zeroes,
 40‚Üí * and the parser expects the field to be at least [minDigits] digits long.
 41‚Üí */
 42‚Üíinternal abstract class UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 43‚Üí    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 44‚Üí    private val minDigits: Int,
 45‚Üí    private val spacePadding: Int?,
 46‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 47‚Üí
 48‚Üí    private val maxDigits: Int = field.maxDigits
 49‚Üí
 50‚Üí    init {
 51‚Üí        require(minDigits >= 0) {
 52‚Üí            "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative"
 53‚Üí        }
 54‚Üí        require(maxDigits >= minDigits) {
 55‚Üí            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 56‚Üí        }
 57‚Üí        if (spacePadding != null) {
 58‚Üí            require(spacePadding > minDigits) {
 59‚Üí                "The space padding ($spacePadding) should be more than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 60‚Üí            }
 61‚Üí        }
 62‚Üí    }
 63‚Üí
 64‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
 65‚Üí        val formatter = UnsignedIntFormatterStructure(
 66‚Üí            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
 67‚Üí            zeroPadding = minDigits,
 68‚Üí        )
 69‚Üí        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
 70‚Üí    }
 71‚Üí
 72‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
 73‚Üí        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, field.accessor, field.name)
 74‚Üí}
 75‚Üí
 76‚Üí/**
 77‚Üí * A directive for a string-based format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 78‚Üí */
 79‚Üíinternal abstract class NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 80‚Üí    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 81‚Üí    private val values: List<String>,
 82‚Üí    private val name: String,
 83‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 84‚Üí
 85‚Üí    init {
 86‚Üí        require(values.size == field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1) {
 87‚Üí            "The number of values (${values.size}) in $values does not match the range of the field (${field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1})"
 88‚Üí        }
 89‚Üí    }
 90‚Üí
 91‚Üí    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
 92‚Üí        values.getOrNull(it-field.minValue)
 93‚Üí            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
 94‚Üí    }
 95‚Üí
 96‚Üí    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
 97‚Üí        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
 98‚Üí            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, values.indexOf(newValue) + field.minValue)?.let {
 99‚Üí                values[it - field.minValue]
100‚Üí            }
101‚Üí
102‚Üí        override val name: String get() = this@NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective.name
103‚Üí    }
104‚Üí
105‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
106‚Üí        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
107‚Üí
108‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
109‚Üí        ParserStructure(
110‚Üí            listOf(
111‚Üí                StringSetParserOperation(values, AssignableString(), "One of $values for $name")
112‚Üí            ), emptyList()
113‚Üí        )
114‚Üí}
115‚Üí
116‚Üí/**
117‚Üí * A directive for a string-based format of an enum field.
118‚Üí */
119‚Üíinternal abstract class NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target, Type>(
120‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Type>,
121‚Üí    private val mapping: Map<Type, String>,
122‚Üí    private val name: String,
123‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
124‚Üí
125‚Üí    private val reverseMapping = mapping.entries.associate { it.value to it.key }
126‚Üí
127‚Üí    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
128‚Üí        mapping[field.accessor.getterNotNull(target)]
129‚Üí            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
130‚Üí    }
131‚Üí
132‚Üí    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
133‚Üí        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
134‚Üí            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, reverseMapping[newValue]!!)?.let { mapping[it] }
135‚Üí
136‚Üí        override val name: String get() = this@NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective.name
137‚Üí    }
138‚Üí
139‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
140‚Üí        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
141‚Üí
142‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
143‚Üí        ParserStructure(
144‚Üí            listOf(
145‚Üí                StringSetParserOperation(mapping.values, AssignableString(), "One of ${mapping.values} for $name")
146‚Üí            ), emptyList()
147‚Üí        )
148‚Üí}

150‚Üíinternal abstract class StringFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
151‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, String>,
152‚Üí    private val acceptedStrings: Set<String>,
153‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
154‚Üí
155‚Üí    init {
156‚Üí        require(acceptedStrings.isNotEmpty()) {
157‚Üí            "The set of accepted strings is empty"
158‚Üí        }
159‚Üí    }
160‚Üí
161‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
162‚Üí        StringFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull)
163‚Üí
164‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
165‚Üí        ParserStructure(
166‚Üí            listOf(StringSetParserOperation(acceptedStrings, field.accessor, field.name)),
167‚Üí            emptyList()
168‚Üí        )
169‚Üí}

171‚Üíinternal abstract class SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
172‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
173‚Üí    private val minDigits: Int?,
174‚Üí    private val maxDigits: Int?,
175‚Üí    private val spacePadding: Int?,
176‚Üí    private val outputPlusOnExceededWidth: Int?,
177‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
178‚Üí
179‚Üí    init {
180‚Üí        require(minDigits == null || minDigits >= 0) { "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative" }
181‚Üí        require(maxDigits == null || minDigits == null || maxDigits >= minDigits) {
182‚Üí            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
183‚Üí        }
184‚Üí    }
185‚Üí
186‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
187‚Üí        val formatter = SignedIntFormatterStructure(
188‚Üí            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
189‚Üí            zeroPadding = minDigits ?: 0,
190‚Üí            outputPlusOnExceededWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
191‚Üí        )
192‚Üí        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
193‚Üí    }
194‚Üí
195‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
196‚Üí        SignedIntParser(
197‚Üí            minDigits = minDigits,
198‚Üí            maxDigits = maxDigits,
199‚Üí            spacePadding = spacePadding,
200‚Üí            field.accessor,
201‚Üí            field.name,
202‚Üí            plusOnExceedsWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
203‚Üí        )
204‚Üí}
205‚Üí
206‚Üíinternal abstract class DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
207‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, DecimalFraction>,
208‚Üí    private val minDigits: Int,
209‚Üí    private val maxDigits: Int,
210‚Üí    private val zerosToAdd: List<Int>,
211‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
212‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
213‚Üí        DecimalFractionFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull, minDigits, maxDigits, zerosToAdd)
214‚Üí
215‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> = ParserStructure(
216‚Üí        listOf(
217‚Üí            NumberSpanParserOperation(
218‚Üí                listOf(FractionPartConsumer(minDigits, maxDigits, field.accessor, field.name))
219‚Üí            )
220‚Üí        ),
221‚Üí        emptyList()
222‚Üí    )
223‚Üí}
224‚Üí
225‚Üíinternal abstract class ReducedIntFieldDirective<in Target>(
226‚Üí    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
227‚Üí    private val digits: Int,
228‚Üí    private val base: Int,
229‚Üí) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
230‚Üí
231‚Üí    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =

238‚Üí    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
239‚Üí        ReducedIntParser(digits = digits, base = base, field.accessor, field.name)
240‚Üí}

 12‚Üí/**

 21‚Üí    /**
 22‚Üí     * Formats the given [value] into the given [appendable] using this format.
 23‚Üí     */
 24‚Üí    public fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A
 25‚Üí
 26‚Üí    /**
 27‚Üí     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 28‚Üí     *
 29‚Üí     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 30‚Üí     */
 31‚Üí    public fun parse(input: CharSequence): T
 32‚Üí
 33‚Üí    /**
 34‚Üí     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 35‚Üí     *
 36‚Üí     * @return the parsed value, or `null` if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 37‚Üí     */
 38‚Üí    public fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T?
 39‚Üí
 40‚Üí    public companion object {
 41‚Üí        /**
 42‚Üí         * Produces Kotlin code that, when pasted into a Kotlin source file, creates a [DateTimeFormat] instance that
 43‚Üí         * behaves identically to [format].
 44‚Üí         *
 45‚Üí         * The typical use case for this is to create a [DateTimeFormat] instance using a non-idiomatic approach and
 46‚Üí         * then convert it to a builder DSL.
 47‚Üí         */
 48‚Üí        public fun formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): String = when (format) {
 49‚Üí            is AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *> -> format.actualFormat.builderString(allFormatConstants)
 50‚Üí        }
 51‚Üí    }
 52‚Üí}
 53‚Üí
 54‚Üí/**
 55‚Üí * The style of padding to use when formatting a value.
 56‚Üí */
 57‚Üípublic enum class Padding {
 58‚Üí    /**
 59‚Üí     * No padding.
 60‚Üí     */
 61‚Üí    NONE,
 62‚Üí
 63‚Üí    /**
 64‚Üí     * Pad with zeros.
 65‚Üí     */
 66‚Üí    ZERO,
 67‚Üí
 68‚Üí    /**
 69‚Üí     * Pad with spaces.
 70‚Üí     */
 71‚Üí    SPACE
 72‚Üí}
 73‚Üí
 74‚Üíinternal fun Padding.toKotlinCode(): String = when (this) {
 75‚Üí    Padding.NONE -> "Padding.NONE"
 76‚Üí    Padding.ZERO -> "Padding.ZERO"
 77‚Üí    Padding.SPACE -> "Padding.SPACE"
 78‚Üí}
 79‚Üí
 80‚Üíinternal inline fun Padding.minDigits(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.ZERO) width else 1
 81‚Üíinternal inline fun Padding.spaces(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.SPACE) width else null
